query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    d.check_status AS [Status],
    COUNT(*) AS [Count]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_check d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY d.check_status

panels.title: CPU Utilization
panels.description: Historical CPU utilization percentage over time from SQLWATCH performance counters.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    instance_name + ' %'  AS [Series],
    AVG(CONVERT(MONEY,d.cntr_value_calculated)) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_performance_counters d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_performance_counter m WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON m.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND m.performance_counter_id = d.performance_counter_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
    AND m.counter_name IN ('Processor Time %','Privileged Time %','User Time %')
    AND m.cntr_type <> 1073939712
GROUP BY h.report_time ,
    instance_name

panels.title: Activity (Batch Requests, Logins, etc.)
panels.description: Batch requests and SQL compilations per second showing workload intensity trends.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    m.counter_name AS [Series],
    SUM(CONVERT(MONEY,d.cntr_value_calculated)) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_performance_counters d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_performance_counter m WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON m.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND m.performance_counter_id = d.performance_counter_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
    AND m.counter_name IN ('Batch Requests/Sec','Logins/sec','Transactions/sec','SQL Compilations/sec','User Connections')
    AND m.cntr_type <> 1073939712
GROUP BY h.report_time,
    m.counter_name

panels.title: Wait Events by Category
panels.description: Wait statistics grouped by category (CPU, I/O, Memory, Lock) showing performance bottleneck areas.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    ISNULL(ws.wait_category, 'Other') AS [Series],
    SUM(d.wait_time_ms_delta) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_wait_stats d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
LEFT JOIN dbo.vw_sqlwatch_meta_wait_stats_category ws WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON ws.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND ws.wait_type_id = d.wait_type_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY h.report_time, ISNULL(ws.wait_category, 'Other')
HAVING SUM(d.wait_time_ms_delta) > 0
ORDER BY h.report_time

panels.title: File Stats - Latency (ms)
panels.description: Database file I/O latency in milliseconds. High values indicate disk performance issues.

query.sqlServer:
;WITH fl AS (

SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    ISNULL(f.database_name,'?') + ' - ' + ISNULL(mf.file_name,'?') AS [Series],
    AVG(CONVERT(MONEY,CASE WHEN d.num_of_reads_delta > 0 THEN d.io_stall_read_ms_delta / d.num_of_reads_delta ELSE 0 END
    + CASE WHEN d.num_of_writes_delta > 0 THEN d.io_stall_write_ms_delta / d.num_of_writes_delta ELSE 0 END)) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_file_stats d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
LEFT JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_master_file mf WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON mf.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND mf.sqlwatch_database_id = d.sqlwatch_database_id
    AND mf.sqlwatch_master_file_id = d.sqlwatch_master_file_id
LEFT JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_database f WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON f.sql_instance = mf.sql_instance
    AND f.sqlwatch_database_id = mf.sqlwatch_database_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY 
h.report_time,
    ISNULL(f.database_name,'?') + ' - ' + ISNULL(mf.file_name,'?')
)


SELECT 
[Time]
,CASE WHEN Rankfilter > 15 THEN 'Other' ELSE fl.[Series] END [Series]
, AVG(fl.[Value]) [Value]
FROM fl
INNER JOIN
(
SELECT 
    
    [Series],
    SUM([Value]) AS [Value]
    ,RANK () OVER(
        --PARTITION BY [Series] 
        ORDER BY SUM([Value]) DESC
    ) as Rankfilter
    FROM fl
GROUP BY [Series]
) ftable ON ftable.Series = fl.Series
GROUP BY 
[Time],
    CASE WHEN Rankfilter > 15 THEN 'Other' ELSE fl.[Series] END

panels.title: File Stats - Throughput (MB/s)
panels.description: Database file I/O throughput in MB/s showing read and write activity.

query.sqlServer:
; WITH dbwl AS
(
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    ISNULL(f.database_name,'?') AS [DB]
    , AVG(CONVERT(MONEY,CASE WHEN ISNULL(d.delta_seconds,0) > 0 THEN d.num_of_bytes_read_delta / d.delta_seconds ELSE 0 END))/1024/1024 AS [Read MB/s]
    , AVG(CONVERT(MONEY,CASE WHEN ISNULL(d.delta_seconds,0) > 0 THEN d.num_of_bytes_written_delta / d.delta_seconds ELSE 0 END))/1024/1024  [Write MB/s]


FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_file_stats d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
LEFT JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_master_file mf WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON mf.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND mf.sqlwatch_database_id = d.sqlwatch_database_id
    AND mf.sqlwatch_master_file_id = d.sqlwatch_master_file_id
LEFT JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_database f WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON f.sql_instance = mf.sql_instance
    AND f.sqlwatch_database_id = mf.sqlwatch_database_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY 
h.report_time ,f.database_name
)

SELECT [Time]
, [Series]
, CASE WHEN filtertable.Rankfilter > 5 THEN REPLACE([Series], T1.[DB], 'Other' ) ELSE [Series] END
,T1.[Value]
, filtertable.Rankfilter
FROM (
SELECT
    [Time],
    [DB],
    ISNULL([DB],'?') + ' Read MB/s' [Series]
    , [Read MB/s] [Value]
FROM dbwl

UNION ALL

SELECT
   [Time],
   [DB],
    ISNULL([DB],'?') + ' Write MB/s' [Series]
    , [Write MB/s] [Value]
FROM dbwl
)
T1 
LEFT OUTER JOIN 
(SELECT 
    
    [DB]
    --SUM([Write MB/s] + [Read MB/s]) AS [Value]
    ,RANK () OVER(
        ORDER BY SUM([Write MB/s] + [Read MB/s]) DESC

    ) as Rankfilter
    FROM dbwl
GROUP BY [DB]
) filtertable ON filtertable.DB = T1.[DB]
ORDER BY T1.[Time]

panels.title: Database Stats
panels.description: Per-database statistics including size, log usage, and transaction activity.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    'Database Count' AS [Series],
    COUNT(DISTINCT d.sqlwatch_database_id) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_disk_utilisation_database d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time > DATEADD(WEEK, -2, GETDATE())
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY h.report_time

UNION ALL

SELECT
    h.report_time,
    'Total Size (GB)',
    CONVERT(MONEY,SUM(ISNULL(d.database_size_bytes,0)) / 1048576.0/1024)
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_disk_utilisation_database d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time > DATEADD(WEEK, -2, GETDATE())
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY h.report_time
ORDER BY [Time]

    'TempDB Size (GB)' AS [Series]

    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_master_file mf WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON mf.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND mf.sqlwatch_database_id = d.sqlwatch_database_id
    AND mf.sqlwatch_master_file_id = d.sqlwatch_master_file_id
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_database db WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON db.sql_instance = mf.sql_instance
    AND db.sqlwatch_database_id = mf.sqlwatch_database_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
    AND db.database_name = 'tempdb'
GROUP BY h.report_time,file_name
) T
GROUP BY [Time],[Series]
ORDER BY [Time]

panels.title: Check Failures Over Time
panels.description: Historical SQLWATCH check results showing pass/fail trends over time.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    d.check_status AS [Series],
    COUNT(*) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_check d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
    AND d.check_status <> 'OK'
GROUP BY h.report_time, d.check_status
ORDER BY h.report_time

SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    m.counter_name AS [Series],
    CONVERT(MONEY,SUM(d.cntr_value_calculated)) AS [Value]
    --, m.*
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_performance_counters d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_performance_counter m WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON m.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND m.performance_counter_id = d.performance_counter_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
    AND m.counter_name IN ('Batch Requests/Sec','Readahead pages/sec','Transactions/sec','Processes blocked')
    AND m.cntr_type <> 1073939712
GROUP BY h.report_time ,
    m.counter_name

panels.title: Key Stats
panels.description: Key performance indicator showing overall instance health status from SQLWATCH checks.

query.sqlServer:
select TOP 1
 --time = pc.snapshot_time
--,
'CPU usage %' = sum(case 
 when 1=1 and mpc.counter_name = 'Processor Time %' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated] 
 --when serverproperty('EngineEdition') = 3 and mpc.counter_name = 'CPU usage %' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated] 
 --when serverproperty('EngineEdition') <> 3 and mpc.counter_name = 'Processor Time %' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]
 else null end)
--, 'Batch Requests/sec' =CONVERT(INT, avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Batch Requests/sec' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated] else null end))

, 'Logins/sec' = CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Logins/sec' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated] else null end))
--, 'Transactions/sec' = CONVERT(INT,sum(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Transactions/sec' and pc.instance_name not in ('_Total','mssqlsystemresource') then pc.[cntr_value_calculated] else null end))
, 'User Connections' = avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'User Connections' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated] else null end)
--, 'SQL Compilations/sec' = CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'SQL Compilations/sec' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated] else null end))
, 'Availability Group Bytes' = avg(case when mpc.counter_name in ('Bytes Sent to Replica/sec','Bytes Received from Replica/sec') then pc.[cntr_value_calculated] else null end)
 ,'Connection Memory (MB)'		= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Connection Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024	else null end))
 ,'Optimizer Memory (MB)'		= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Optimizer Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024		else null end))
 ,'SQL Cache Memory (MB)'		= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'SQL Cache Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024		else null end))
 ,'Total Server Memory (MB)'	= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Total Server Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024	else null end))
 ,'Stolen Server Memory (MB)'	= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Stolen Server Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024 else null end))
 ,'Target Server Memory (MB)'	= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Target Server Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024 else null end))
from [dbo].[sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_performance_counters] pc

inner join [dbo].[sqlwatch_meta_performance_counter] mpc
on pc.sql_instance = mpc.sql_instance
and pc.performance_counter_id = mpc.performance_counter_id

WHERE snapshot_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo

and pc.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
and mpc.counter_name in ('CPU usage %'
 ,'Batch Requests/sec'
 ,'Logins/sec'
 ,'Transactions/sec'
 ,'User Connections'
 ,'SQL Compilations/sec'
 ,'Bytes Sent to Replica/sec'
 ,'Bytes Received from Replica/sec'
 ,'Processor Time %'
 ,'% Processor Time'
 
 ,'Connection Memory (KB)'
 ,'Optimizer Memory (KB)'
 ,'SQL Cache Memory (KB)'
 ,'Total Server Memory (KB)'
 ,'Stolen Server Memory (KB)'
 , 'Target Server Memory (KB)'
 )

group by pc.snapshot_time
order by pc.snapshot_time DESC

SELECT [Time]=event_time, [Series] = 'Long queries', [Value] = count(*)
FROM [dbo].[vw_sqlwatch_report_fact_xes_long_queries]
WHERE report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY event_time
ORDER BY  [Time] desc

panels.title: Long Queries Details
panels.description: Detailed metrics for long-running queries including duration, CPU, reads, writes, and execution count from SQLWATCH query snapshots.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT *  
FROM [dbo].[vw_sqlwatch_report_fact_xes_long_queries]
WHERE report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
ORDER BY  event_time desc

panels.title: Query Text
panels.description: Full query text and execution plan XML for selected long-running queries. Enables deep-dive analysis of problematic queries.

query.sqlServer:

FROM [dbo].[vw_sqlwatch_report_fact_xes_long_queries]

panels.title: Wait Events by Category
panels.description: Wait time trends by category (CPU, I/O, Memory, Lock, Network) over time. Identifies shifting performance bottlenecks.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    ISNULL(ws.wait_category, 'Other') AS [Series],
    SUM(d.wait_time_ms_delta) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_wait_stats d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
LEFT JOIN dbo.vw_sqlwatch_meta_wait_stats_category ws WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON ws.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND ws.wait_type_id = d.wait_type_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY h.report_time, ISNULL(ws.wait_category, 'Other')
HAVING SUM(d.wait_time_ms_delta) > 0
ORDER BY h.report_time

panels.title: Wait Event Details
panels.description: Detailed wait statistics by wait type showing wait time, wait count, and percentage of total waits from SQLWATCH snapshots.

query.sqlServer:
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; 
SELECT TOP 250
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    ISNULL(ws.wait_category, 'Other') AS [Category],
    mw.wait_type AS [Wait Type],
    SUM(d.wait_time_ms_delta) AS [Wait Time (ms)],
    SUM(d.waiting_tasks_count_delta) AS [Waiting Tasks],
    d.sql_instance
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_wait_stats d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
LEFT JOIN dbo.vw_sqlwatch_meta_wait_stats_category ws WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON ws.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND ws.wait_type_id = d.wait_type_id
LEFT JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_wait_stats mw WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON mw.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND mw.wait_type_id = d.wait_type_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
    AND d.wait_time_ms_delta > 0
GROUP BY h.report_time, ISNULL(ws.wait_category, 'Other'), mw.wait_type, d.sql_instance
ORDER BY h.report_time DESC, [Wait Time (ms)] DESC

panels.title: CPU %
panels.description: Current CPU utilization percentage from ring buffer. Shows ProcessUtilization from sys.dm_os_ring_buffers RING_BUFFER_SCHEDULER_MONITOR.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 1 CONVERT(XML,record).value('(./Record/SchedulerMonitorEvent/SystemHealth/ProcessUtilization)[1]','int') AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_ring_buffers WITH(NOLOCK) WHERE ring_buffer_type='RING_BUFFER_SCHEDULER_MONITOR' ORDER BY timestamp DESC

panels.title: Batch Req
panels.description: Batch requests per second. Measures SQL Server workload intensity from sys.dm_os_performance_counters.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ISNULL(SUM(CONVERT(BIGINT,cntr_value)),0) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WITH(NOLOCK) WHERE LOWER(object_name) LIKE '%sql statistics%' AND LOWER(counter_name)='batch requests/sec'

panels.title: Transactions
panels.description: Active transactions per second. Tracks transaction throughput from sys.dm_os_performance_counters.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ISNULL(SUM(CONVERT(BIGINT,cntr_value)),0) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WITH(NOLOCK) WHERE LOWER(object_name) LIKE '%databases%' AND LOWER(counter_name)='transactions/sec' AND LOWER(instance_name)<>'_total'

panels.title: Compilations
panels.description: SQL compilations and recompilations per second. High values may indicate plan cache issues or missing parameterization.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ISNULL(SUM(CONVERT(BIGINT,cntr_value)),0) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WITH(NOLOCK) WHERE LOWER(object_name) LIKE '%sql statistics%' AND LOWER(counter_name)='sql compilations/sec'

panels.title: Page Reads
panels.description: Page reads per second from disk. High values indicate memory pressure or missing indexes forcing disk I/O.

query.sqlServer: SELECT @@TOTAL_READ AS [Value]

panels.title: Page Writes
panels.description: Page writes per second to disk. Tracks checkpoint and lazy writer activity.

query.sqlServer: SELECT @@TOTAL_WRITE AS [Value]

panels.title: Poison Waits
panels.description: Cumulative poison wait types (CXPACKET, PAGEIOLATCH, etc.) that indicate performance bottlenecks. Sourced from sys.dm_os_wait_stats.

query.sqlServer: SELECT SUM(CONVERT(BIGINT,CASE WHEN wait_type IN('IO_QUEUE_LIMIT', 'IO_RETRY', 'LOG_RATE_GOVERNOR', 'POOL_LOG_RATE_GOVERNOR', 'RESMGR_THROTTLED', 'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE', 'THREADPOOL') THEN wait_time_ms-signal_wait_time_ms ELSE 0 END)) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WITH(NOLOCK)

panels.title: Serializable Locking
panels.description: Lock requests per second at SERIALIZABLE isolation level. High values may indicate blocking or deadlock risks.

query.sqlServer: SELECT SUM(CONVERT(BIGINT,CASE WHEN wait_type IN('LCK_M_RS_S', 'LCK_M_RS_U', 'LCK_M_RIn_NL','LCK_M_RIn_S', 'LCK_M_RIn_U','LCK_M_RIn_X', 'LCK_M_RX_S', 'LCK_M_RX_U','LCK_M_RX_X') THEN wait_time_ms-signal_wait_time_ms ELSE 0 END)) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WITH(NOLOCK)

panels.title: CMEMTHREAD NUMA/
panels.description: CMEMTHREAD wait time indicating memory grant queue waits. Suggests queries waiting for memory to execute.

query.sqlServer: SELECT SUM(CONVERT(BIGINT,CASE WHEN wait_type = 'CMEMTHREAD' THEN wait_time_ms-signal_wait_time_ms ELSE 0 END)) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WITH(NOLOCK)

panels.title: Active Sessions
panels.description: Count of active user sessions from sys.dm_exec_sessions where is_user_process = 1.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP (@TopRows) s.spid AS Spid, DB_NAME(s.dbid) AS [Database], status AS Status, CAST(cpu AS BIGINT) AS Cpu, CAST(physical_io AS BIGINT) AS PhysicalIo, program_name AS ProgramName FROM sys.sysprocesses s WHERE s.spid > 50 AND program_name NOT LIKE '%SQLMonitorUI%' AND hostname <> '' AND program_name <> '' ORDER BY (ISNULL(cpu,0) + ISNULL(physical_io,0)) DESC

panels.title: Blocking Chains
panels.description: Number of blocked sessions from sys.dm_exec_requests where blocking_session_id > 0.

query.sqlServer: SELECT l.request_session_id AS WaitSpid, DB_NAME(l.resource_database_id) AS DatabaseName, l.resource_type AS LockType, wt.blocking_session_id AS BlockerSpid, wt.wait_type AS WaitType, wt.wait_duration_ms AS WaitDurationMs, ISNULL(wst.text, '') AS WaitStatement, ISNULL(bst.text, '') AS BlockerStatement FROM sys.dm_tran_locks l WITH (NOLOCK) JOIN sys.dm_os_waiting_tasks wt WITH (NOLOCK) ON l.lock_owner_address = wt.resource_address OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text((SELECT sql_handle FROM sys.dm_exec_requests WHERE session_id = l.request_session_id)) wst OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text((SELECT sql_handle FROM sys.dm_exec_requests WHERE session_id = wt.blocking_session_id)) bst WHERE wt.blocking_session_id IS NOT NULL ORDER BY wt.wait_duration_ms DESC

panels.title: Recent Deadlocks
panels.description: Deadlock events captured from the system_health extended event session. Shows process details, lock resources, and SQL text.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 20 xed.value('(@timestamp)', 'DATETIME2') AS event_time, xed.query('.') AS deadlock_xml FROM (SELECT CAST(target_data AS XML) AS target_data FROM sys.dm_xe_session_targets st JOIN sys.dm_xe_sessions s ON s.address = st.event_session_address WHERE s.name = 'system_health' AND st.target_name = 'ring_buffer') AS data CROSS APPLY target_data.nodes('RingBufferTarget/event[@name="xml_deadlock_report"]') AS xevent(xed) ORDER BY event_time DESC

panels.title: Top Usage Queries
panels.description: Top active sessions by CPU, reads, or duration. Shows session_id, login, database, and current query text.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    TMP.Category,
    [Source],
    DB_NAME(st.dbid) [DB],
    TMP.[Total Elapsed Time in S],
    TMP.[Total Execution Count],
    TMP.[Total CPU Time in S],
    TMP.[Total Logical Reads],
    TMP.[Total Logical Writes],
    TMP.[Total CLR Time],
    TMP.[Number of Statements],
    TMP.[Last Execution Time],
    TMP.[Plan Handle],
    st.text AS [Query],
    qp.query_plan AS [Plan]
FROM (
    SELECT * FROM (
        SELECT TOP 15 'worst I/O' [Category],
            'Query' [Source],
            CAST(SUM(s.total_elapsed_time) / 1000000.0 AS DECIMAL(20, 2)) AS [Total Elapsed Time in S],
            SUM(s.execution_count) AS [Total Execution Count],
            CAST(SUM(s.total_worker_time) / 1000000.0 AS DECIMAL(20, 2)) AS [Total CPU Time in S],
            SUM(s.total_logical_reads) AS [Total Logical Reads],
            SUM(s.total_logical_writes) AS [Total Logical Writes],
            SUM(s.total_clr_time) AS [Total CLR Time],
            COUNT(1) AS [Number of Statements],
            MAX(s.last_execution_time) AS [Last Execution Time],
            s.plan_handle AS [Plan Handle]
        FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats s WITH (NOLOCK)
        GROUP BY s.plan_handle ORDER BY SUM(s.total_logical_reads + s.total_logical_writes) DESC
    ) QS
    UNION ALL
        SELECT * FROM (
        SELECT TOP 15 'worst I/O' [Category],
            'Procedure' [Source],
            CAST(SUM(s.total_elapsed_time) / 1000000.0 AS DECIMAL(20, 2)) AS [Total Elapsed Time in S],
            SUM(s.execution_count) AS [Total Execution Count],
            CAST(SUM(s.total_worker_time) / 1000000.0 AS DECIMAL(20, 2)) AS [Total CPU Time in S],
            SUM(s.total_logical_reads) AS [Total Logical Reads],
            SUM(s.total_logical_writes) AS [Total Logical Writes],
            SUM(0e) AS [Total CLR Time],
            COUNT(1) AS [Number of Statements],
            MAX(s.last_execution_time) AS [Last Execution Time],
            s.plan_handle AS [Plan Handle]
        FROM sys.dm_exec_procedure_stats s WITH (NOLOCK)
        GROUP BY s.plan_handle ORDER BY SUM(s.total_logical_reads + s.total_logical_writes) DESC
    ) PS
) TMP
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(TMP.[Plan Handle]) AS st
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_query_plan(TMP.[Plan Handle]) AS qp
ORDER BY Category, [Total Logical Reads] DESC
OPTION (MAXDOP 1)

panels.title: Disk Space
panels.description: Database file space usage and growth trends. Monitors disk capacity and alerts on low space conditions.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT DISTINCT
    vs.volume_mount_point AS [Label]
    ,100-CAST((CAST(vs.available_bytes AS FLOAT) / CAST(vs.total_bytes AS FLOAT)) * 100 AS DECIMAL(18,2)) [Value]
    , '%' AS [Unit]
    , @@SERVERNAME [Instance]
FROM sys.master_files AS f
CROSS APPLY sys.dm_os_volume_stats(f.database_id, f.file_id) AS vs;

panels.title: Check Details
panels.description: Detailed SQLWATCH check results with failure reasons and recommended actions.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    d.snapshot_time,
    c.check_name,
    c.check_description,
    d.check_value,
    d.check_status,
    d.check_exec_time_ms,
    d.sql_instance
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_check d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_check c WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON c.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND c.check_id = d.check_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
ORDER BY d.snapshot_time DESC

SELECT COUNT(*) AS [Value]
FROM sys.databases WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE state_desc = 'ONLINE'

SELECT TOP 1
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    'Memory %' AS [Series],
    d.memory_utilization_percentage AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_process_memory d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
ORDER BY h.report_time DESC

SELECT TOP 1
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    'Pending' AS [Series],
    d.work_queue_count AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_schedulers d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
ORDER BY h.report_time DESC

SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT sql_instance) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_config_sql_instance WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE repo_collector_is_active = 1

SELECT 
    @@SERVERNAME AS ServerName,
    --SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') AS Edition,
    CASE ServerProperty('EngineEdition')
 
WHEN 1  THEN 'Desktop'
WHEN 2  THEN 
    CASE 
    WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(50),SERVERPROPERTY('Edition')) LIKE '%Busi%' THEN 'Business Intelligence'
    WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(50),SERVERPROPERTY('Edition')) LIKE '%Developer%' THEN 'Developer'
    ELSE 'Standard'-- (For Standard, Standard Developer, Web, and Business Intelligence.)
    END
WHEN 3  THEN 
-- (For Enterprise, Enterprise Developer, Developer, and Evaluation editions.)
    CASE 
    WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(50),SERVERPROPERTY('Edition')) LIKE '%Developer%' THEN 'Developer'
    WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(50),SERVERPROPERTY('Edition')) LIKE '%Evaluation%' THEN 'Evaluation'
    ELSE 'Enterprise'
    END

WHEN 4  THEN 'Express'-- (For Express, Express with Tools, and Express with Advanced Services)
WHEN 5  THEN 'SQL Database'
WHEN 6  THEN 'Azure Synapse'-- Analytics
WHEN 8  THEN 'Azure SQL MI'
WHEN 9  THEN 'Azure SQL Edge'-- (For all editions of Azure SQL Edge)
WHEN 11 THEN  'Azure Synapse serverless'-- SQL pool, or Microsoft Fabric
WHEN 12 THEN  'Microsoft Fabric'-- SQL database in Microsoft Fabric.
    END AS Edition
    ,
    (SELECT cpu_count FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) AS VirtualCPUs,
    (SELECT socket_count FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) AS Sockets,
    CASE WHEN (SELECT virtual_machine_type FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) = 1 THEN 'VM' ELSE 'Physical' END AS VMType,
    CAST((SELECT physical_memory_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS MemoryGB,
    CAST((SELECT committed_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS SqlAllocated,
    CAST((SELECT committed_target_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS UsedBySql,
    '' AS MemoryState,
    CASE 
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '8%'    THEN '2000'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '9%'    THEN '2005'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '10.0%' THEN '2008'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '10.5%' THEN '2008 R2'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '11%'   THEN '2012'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '12%'   THEN '2014'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '13%'   THEN '2016'     
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '14%'   THEN '2017' 
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '15%'   THEN '2019' 
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '16%'   THEN '2022' 
     ELSE 'unknown'
  END 
    --@@VERSION 
    AS [Version],
    SERVERPROPERTY('ProductVersion') AS BuildNr,
    (SELECT windows_release FROM sys.dm_os_windows_info) AS OS,
    CASE WHEN SERVERPROPERTY('IsHadrEnabled') = 1 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS HADR,
    CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals WHERE name = 'sa' AND is_disabled = 0) THEN 'Enabled' ELSE 'Disabled' END AS SA,
    '' AS [Level]

SELECT 
    
    (SELECT socket_count FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) AS [CPU Sockets],
    (SELECT cpu_count FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) AS [Virtual CPU Count],
    CASE WHEN (SELECT virtual_machine_type FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) = 1 THEN 'VM' ELSE 'Physical' END AS [System Type],
    (SELECT windows_release FROM sys.dm_os_windows_info) AS [Operating System]

SELECT 
ServerName
, Edition
, '$ ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(25),CONVERT(INT,CONVERT(MONEY,
CASE Edition 
WHEN 'Business Intelligence'
THEN 5434  * VirtualCPUs / 2
  
WHEN 'Standard'
THEN 5434  * VirtualCPUs / 2/ 3.832
  
WHEN 'Enterprise'
THEN 5434  * VirtualCPUs / 2
ELSE  0
END)))
[licensingRetailUSD]
, [Version]
, BuildNr
, HADR
, SA [sa state]

FROM (
SELECT 
    @@SERVERNAME AS ServerName,
    --SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') AS Edition,
    CASE ServerProperty('EngineEdition')
 
WHEN 1  THEN 'Desktop'
WHEN 2  THEN 
    CASE 
    WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(50),SERVERPROPERTY('Edition')) LIKE '%Busi%' THEN 'Business Intelligence'
    WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(50),SERVERPROPERTY('Edition')) LIKE '%Developer%' THEN 'Developer'
    ELSE 'Standard'-- (For Standard, Standard Developer, Web, and Business Intelligence.)
    END
WHEN 3  THEN 
-- (For Enterprise, Enterprise Developer, Developer, and Evaluation editions.)
    CASE 
    WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(50),SERVERPROPERTY('Edition')) LIKE '%Developer%' THEN 'Developer'
    WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(50),SERVERPROPERTY('Edition')) LIKE '%Evaluation%' THEN 'Evaluation'
    ELSE 'Enterprise'
    END

WHEN 4  THEN 'Express'-- (For Express, Express with Tools, and Express with Advanced Services)
WHEN 5  THEN 'SQL Database'
WHEN 6  THEN 'Azure Synapse'-- Analytics
WHEN 8  THEN 'Azure SQL MI'
WHEN 9  THEN 'Azure SQL Edge'-- (For all editions of Azure SQL Edge)
WHEN 11 THEN  'Azure Synapse serverless'-- SQL pool, or Microsoft Fabric
WHEN 12 THEN  'Microsoft Fabric'-- SQL database in Microsoft Fabric.
    END AS Edition
    

    ,
    (SELECT cpu_count FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) AS VirtualCPUs,
    (SELECT socket_count FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) AS Sockets,
    CASE WHEN (SELECT virtual_machine_type FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) = 1 THEN 'VM' ELSE 'Physical' END AS VMType,
    CAST((SELECT physical_memory_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS MemoryGB,
    CAST((SELECT committed_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS SqlAllocated,
    CAST((SELECT committed_target_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS UsedBySql,
    '' AS MemoryState,
    CASE 
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '8%'    THEN '2000'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '9%'    THEN '2005'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '10.0%' THEN '2008'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '10.5%' THEN '2008 R2'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '11%'   THEN '2012'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '12%'   THEN '2014'
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '13%'   THEN '2016'     
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '14%'   THEN '2017' 
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '15%'   THEN '2019' 
     WHEN CONVERT(VARCHAR(128), SERVERPROPERTY ('productversion')) like '16%'   THEN '2022' 
     ELSE 'unknown'
  END 
    --@@VERSION 
    AS [Version],
    SERVERPROPERTY('ProductVersion') AS BuildNr,
    (SELECT windows_release FROM sys.dm_os_windows_info) AS OS,
    CASE WHEN SERVERPROPERTY('IsHadrEnabled') = 1 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS HADR,
    CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals WHERE name = 'sa' AND is_disabled = 0) THEN 'Enabled' ELSE 'Disabled' END AS SA,
    '' AS [Level]
) T1

panels.title: More Key Stats
panels.description: Secondary health indicator tracking critical performance thresholds.

query.sqlServer:
select TOP 1
 'Connection Memory (MB)'		= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Connection Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024	else null end))
 ,'Optimizer Memory (MB)'		= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Optimizer Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024		else null end))
 ,'SQL Cache Memory (MB)'		= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'SQL Cache Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024		else null end))
 ,'Total Server Memory (MB)'	= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Total Server Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024	else null end))
 ,'Stolen Server Memory (MB)'	= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Stolen Server Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024 else null end))
 ,'Target Server Memory (MB)'	= CONVERT(INT,avg(case when mpc.counter_name = 'Target Server Memory (KB)' then pc.[cntr_value_calculated]/1024 else null end))
from [dbo].[sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_performance_counters] pc

inner join [dbo].[sqlwatch_meta_performance_counter] mpc
on pc.sql_instance = mpc.sql_instance
and pc.performance_counter_id = mpc.performance_counter_id

WHERE 1=1--snapshot_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo

and pc.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
and mpc.counter_name in ('CPU usage %'
 ,'Batch Requests/sec'
 ,'Logins/sec'
 ,'Transactions/sec'
 ,'User Connections'
 ,'SQL Compilations/sec'
 ,'Bytes Sent to Replica/sec'
 ,'Bytes Received from Replica/sec'
 ,'Processor Time %'
 ,'% Processor Time'
 
 ,'Connection Memory (KB)'
 ,'Optimizer Memory (KB)'
 ,'SQL Cache Memory (KB)'
 ,'Total Server Memory (KB)'
 ,'Stolen Server Memory (KB)'
 , 'Target Server Memory (KB)'
 )

group by pc.snapshot_time
order by pc.snapshot_time DESC

SELECT 
   
    CAST((SELECT physical_memory_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS [System Total Memory GB],
    CAST((SELECT committed_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS [Memory Allocated to SQL],
    CAST((SELECT committed_target_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS [Memory Used By SQL],
    CONVERT(INT,
    CAST((SELECT committed_target_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2))
    /
    CAST((SELECT committed_kb FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2))*100)
    [SQL Memory usage %]

panels.title: Instances
panels.description: Total count of SQL Server instances being monitored by SQLWATCH.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT sql_instance) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_config_sql_instance WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE repo_collector_is_active = 1

panels.title: SQL Memory
panels.description: SQL Server process memory usage across all monitored instances.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT TOP 1
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    'Memory %' AS [Series],
    d.memory_utilization_percentage AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_process_memory d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
ORDER BY h.report_time DESC

panels.title: Pending Queue
panels.description: Scheduler activity and runnable task counts aggregated across instances.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT TOP 1
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    'Pending' AS [Series],
    d.work_queue_count AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_schedulers d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
ORDER BY h.report_time DESC

panels.title: Health Checks
panels.description: SQLWATCH health check status across all instances showing pass/fail/warning counts.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    d.check_status AS [Status],
    COUNT(*) AS [Count]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_check d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY d.check_status

panels.title: CPU Utilization
panels.description: CPU utilization trends across all monitored instances.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    m.counter_name AS [Series],
    d.cntr_value_calculated AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_performance_counters d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_performance_counter m WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON m.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND m.performance_counter_id = d.performance_counter_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
    AND m.counter_name IN ('Processor Time %','Privileged Time %','User Time %')
    AND m.cntr_type <> 1073939712
ORDER BY h.report_time

panels.title: Performance Counters
panels.description: Key performance counter metrics aggregated from all instances.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    m.counter_name AS [Series],
    d.cntr_value_calculated AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_performance_counters d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_meta_performance_counter m WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON m.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
    AND m.performance_counter_id = d.performance_counter_id
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
    AND m.counter_name IN ('Batch Requests/Sec','Readahead pages/sec','Transactions/sec','Processes blocked')
    AND m.cntr_type <> 1073939712
ORDER BY h.report_time

panels.title: Wait Stats
panels.description: Wait statistics summary across all monitored instances.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    'Wait Time (ms)' AS [Series],
    SUM(d.wait_time_ms_delta) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_os_wait_stats d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY h.report_time
ORDER BY h.report_time

panels.title: File IO Throughput
panels.description: Database file I/O statistics aggregated across instances.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    'Bytes Read/s' AS [Series],
    SUM(CASE WHEN ISNULL(d.delta_seconds,0) > 0 THEN d.num_of_bytes_read_delta / d.delta_seconds ELSE 0 END) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_file_stats d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY h.report_time

UNION ALL

SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    'Bytes Written/s' AS [Series],
    SUM(CASE WHEN ISNULL(d.delta_seconds,0) > 0 THEN d.num_of_bytes_written_delta / d.delta_seconds ELSE 0 END) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_perf_file_stats d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY h.report_time
ORDER BY [Time]

panels.title: Long Running Queries
panels.description: Long-running queries detected across all monitored instances.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    h.report_time AS [Time],
    'Long Queries' AS [Series],
    COUNT(*) AS [Value]
FROM dbo.sqlwatch_logger_xes_long_queries d WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN dbo.sqlwatch_logger_snapshot_header h WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON h.snapshot_time = d.snapshot_time
    AND h.snapshot_type_id = d.snapshot_type_id
    AND h.sql_instance = d.sql_instance
WHERE h.report_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo
    AND d.sql_instance IN (SELECT value FROM STRING_SPLIT(@SqlInstance, ','))
GROUP BY h.report_time
ORDER BY h.report_time

panels.title: Live Sessions Bubble View
panels.description: Interactive session bubble view showing active sessions positioned by CPU time (Y-axis) and logical reads/writes (X-axis). Bubble size indicates activity score. Click bubbles for session details.

query.sqlServer: SELECT s.session_id AS SPID, s.login_name AS LoginName, ISNULL(s.host_name, '') AS HostName, ISNULL(DB_NAME(s.database_id), '') AS DatabaseName, s.status AS SessionStatus, s.cpu_time AS CpuTime, s.reads AS LogicalReads, s.writes AS Writes, s.open_transaction_count AS OpenTransactionCount, r.status AS RequestStatus, r.command AS Command, r.wait_type AS WaitType, ISNULL(r.wait_time, 0) AS WaitTime, ISNULL(r.blocking_session_id, 0) AS BlockingSessionId, ISNULL(r.total_elapsed_time, 0) AS TotalElapsedTime, ISNULL(s.program_name, '') AS ProgramName, t.text AS QueryText FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions s WITH (NOLOCK) LEFT JOIN sys.dm_exec_requests r WITH (NOLOCK) ON s.session_id = r.session_id OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(r.sql_handle) t WHERE s.is_user_process = 1 ORDER BY s.cpu_time + s.reads DESC

panels.title: Top CPU Queries (last hour)
panels.description: Top queries by total CPU time from Query Store. Shows query_id, execution count, avg CPU, and query text.

query.sqlServer:
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 500
    q.query_id AS [Query ID],
    LEFT(qt.query_sql_text, 150) AS [Query (truncated)],
    ROUND(AVG(rs.avg_cpu_time) / 1000.0, 1) AS [Avg CPU (ms)],
    ROUND(AVG(rs.avg_duration) / 1000.0, 1) AS [Avg Duration (ms)],
    SUM(rs.count_executions) AS [Executions],
    ROUND(AVG(rs.avg_logical_io_reads), 0) AS [Avg Logical Reads]
FROM sys.query_store_query q WITH (NOLOCK)
JOIN sys.query_store_query_text qt WITH (NOLOCK) ON q.query_text_id = qt.query_text_id
JOIN sys.query_store_plan p WITH (NOLOCK) ON q.query_id = p.query_id
JOIN sys.query_store_runtime_stats rs WITH (NOLOCK) ON p.plan_id = rs.plan_id
JOIN sys.query_store_runtime_stats_interval rsi WITH (NOLOCK) ON rs.runtime_stats_interval_id = rsi.runtime_stats_interval_id
WHERE rsi.start_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -1, GETUTCDATE())
  AND q.is_internal_query = 0
GROUP BY q.query_id, qt.query_sql_text
ORDER BY AVG(rs.avg_cpu_time) DESC

panels.title: Top Duration Queries (last hour)
panels.description: Top queries by total duration from Query Store. Identifies long-running queries impacting user experience.

query.sqlServer:
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 500
    q.query_id AS [Query ID],
    LEFT(qt.query_sql_text, 150) AS [Query (truncated)],
    ROUND(AVG(rs.avg_duration) / 1000.0, 1) AS [Avg Duration (ms)],
    ROUND(AVG(rs.avg_cpu_time) / 1000.0, 1) AS [Avg CPU (ms)],
    SUM(rs.count_executions) AS [Executions],
    ROUND(AVG(rs.avg_logical_io_reads), 0) AS [Avg Logical Reads],
    ROUND(AVG(rs.avg_physical_io_reads), 0) AS [Avg Physical Reads]
FROM sys.query_store_query q WITH (NOLOCK)
JOIN sys.query_store_query_text qt WITH (NOLOCK) ON q.query_text_id = qt.query_text_id
JOIN sys.query_store_plan p WITH (NOLOCK) ON q.query_id = p.query_id
JOIN sys.query_store_runtime_stats rs WITH (NOLOCK) ON p.plan_id = rs.plan_id
JOIN sys.query_store_runtime_stats_interval rsi WITH (NOLOCK) ON rs.runtime_stats_interval_id = rsi.runtime_stats_interval_id
WHERE rsi.start_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -1, GETUTCDATE())
  AND q.is_internal_query = 0
GROUP BY q.query_id, qt.query_sql_text
ORDER BY AVG(rs.avg_duration) DESC

panels.title: High Plan Variation
panels.description: Queries with multiple execution plans showing plan_id variations. Helps identify parameter sniffing or plan instability.

query.sqlServer:
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 500
    q.query_id AS [Query ID],
    LEFT(qt.query_sql_text, 150) AS [Query (truncated)],
    COUNT(DISTINCT p.plan_id) AS [Plan Count],
    ROUND(MAX(rs.avg_duration) / 1000.0, 1) AS [Max Avg Dur (ms)],
    ROUND(MIN(rs.avg_duration) / 1000.0, 1) AS [Min Avg Dur (ms)],
    ROUND((MAX(rs.avg_duration) - MIN(rs.avg_duration)) / 1000.0, 1) AS [Variance (ms)]
FROM sys.query_store_query q WITH (NOLOCK)
JOIN sys.query_store_query_text qt WITH (NOLOCK) ON q.query_text_id = qt.query_text_id
JOIN sys.query_store_plan p WITH (NOLOCK) ON q.query_id = p.query_id
JOIN sys.query_store_runtime_stats rs WITH (NOLOCK) ON p.plan_id = rs.plan_id
WHERE q.is_internal_query = 0
GROUP BY q.query_id, qt.query_sql_text
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.plan_id) >= 2
ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT p.plan_id) DESC, (MAX(rs.avg_duration) - MIN(rs.avg_duration)) DESC

panels.title: Regressed Queries
panels.description: Regressed queries where recent performance is worse than historical baseline. Compares recent vs overall avg duration.

query.sqlServer:
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 500
    q.query_id AS [Query ID],
    LEFT(qt.query_sql_text, 150) AS [Query (truncated)],
    ROUND(recent.avg_duration / 1000.0, 1) AS [Recent Avg Dur (ms)],
    ROUND(hist.avg_duration / 1000.0, 1) AS [Historical Avg Dur (ms)],
    ROUND((recent.avg_duration - hist.avg_duration) / NULLIF(hist.avg_duration, 0) * 100, 0) AS [Regression %],
    recent.count_executions AS [Recent Executions]
FROM sys.query_store_query q WITH (NOLOCK)
JOIN sys.query_store_query_text qt WITH (NOLOCK) ON q.query_text_id = qt.query_text_id
CROSS APPLY (
    SELECT AVG(CAST(rs.avg_duration AS BIGINT)) AS avg_duration, SUM(rs.count_executions) AS count_executions
    FROM sys.query_store_plan p WITH (NOLOCK)
    JOIN sys.query_store_runtime_stats rs WITH (NOLOCK) ON p.plan_id = rs.plan_id
    JOIN sys.query_store_runtime_stats_interval rsi WITH (NOLOCK) ON rs.runtime_stats_interval_id = rsi.runtime_stats_interval_id
    WHERE p.query_id = q.query_id AND rsi.start_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -1, GETUTCDATE())
) recent
CROSS APPLY (
    SELECT AVG(CAST(rs.avg_duration AS BIGINT)) AS avg_duration
    FROM sys.query_store_plan p WITH (NOLOCK)
    JOIN sys.query_store_runtime_stats rs WITH (NOLOCK) ON p.plan_id = rs.plan_id
    JOIN sys.query_store_runtime_stats_interval rsi WITH (NOLOCK) ON rs.runtime_stats_interval_id = rsi.runtime_stats_interval_id
    WHERE p.query_id = q.query_id
      AND rsi.start_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETUTCDATE())
      AND rsi.start_time < DATEADD(HOUR, -1, GETUTCDATE())
) hist
WHERE q.is_internal_query = 0
  AND recent.avg_duration > hist.avg_duration * 1.5
  AND hist.avg_duration > 10000
ORDER BY (recent.avg_duration - hist.avg_duration) / NULLIF(hist.avg_duration, 0) DESC

panels.title: Latch Stats
panels.description: Latch wait statistics showing contention on internal SQL Server data structures. High latch waits indicate internal bottlenecks.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ls.collection_time AS [Time], ls.latch_class AS [Series], ls.wait_time_ms AS [Value] FROM collect.latch_stats AS ls WHERE ls.collection_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo ORDER BY ls.collection_time

panels.title: Spinlock Stats
panels.description: Spinlock statistics showing lightweight synchronization contention. Useful for diagnosing high CPU with low query activity.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ss.collection_time AS [Time], ss.spinlock_name AS [Series], ss.backoffs AS [Value] FROM collect.spinlock_stats AS ss WHERE ss.collection_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo ORDER BY ss.collection_time

panels.title: Memory Grants
panels.description: Memory grant requests and waits. Shows queries waiting for memory to execute and grant sizes.

query.sqlServer: SELECT mgs.collection_time AS [Time], 'Granted Memory (MB)' AS [Series], mgs.granted_memory_mb AS [Value] FROM collect.memory_grant_stats AS mgs WHERE mgs.collection_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo ORDER BY mgs.collection_time

panels.title: Plan Cache
panels.description: Plan cache statistics including cache size, hit ratio, and plan reuse metrics.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT pcs.[cacheobjtype] AS [Label]
, 'MB' [Unit]
, SUM(pcs.[avg_size_kb] ) / 1024.0 AS [Value] 
, @@SERVERNAME [Instance]
FROM collect.plan_cache_stats AS pcs  
WHERE pcs.collection_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo 
GROUP BY pcs.cacheobjtype

panels.title: TempDB Usage
panels.description: TempDB usage by session and object type. Identifies queries causing tempdb pressure.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ts.collection_time AS [Time], 'User Objects (MB)' AS [Series], ts.user_object_reserved_page_count * 8.0 / 1024 AS [Value] FROM collect.tempdb_stats AS ts WHERE ts.collection_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo ORDER BY ts.collection_time

panels.title: Configuration Changes
panels.description: SQL Server configuration changes tracked over time from default trace.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT TOP 50 scc.change_time AS [Time], scc.configuration_name AS [Setting]
, scc.[new_value_in_use] AS [New Value]
, scc.[old_value_in_use] AS [Old Value] 
FROM report.server_configuration_changes AS scc WHERE scc.change_time BETWEEN @TimeFrom AND @TimeTo ORDER BY scc.change_time DESC

panels.title: Total Memory Usage
panels.description: Total SQL Server memory usage including committed and target memory from sys.dm_os_sys_info.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST( (SELECT SUM(pages_kb) FROM sys.dm_os_memory_clerks) / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS [Value]

panels.title: Buffer Pool
panels.description: Buffer pool memory allocation and page life expectancy trends.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(SUM(pages_kb) / 1024.0 AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_memory_clerks WHERE type = 'CACHESTORE_BUFPOOL'

panels.title: Memory Clerks
panels.description: Memory distribution by clerk type showing how SQL Server allocates memory across components.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 50 type, SUM(pages_kb) AS pages_kb FROM sys.dm_os_memory_clerks GROUP BY type ORDER BY SUM(pages_kb) DESC

panels.title: Default Trace Events
panels.description: Events from default trace including auto-grow, auto-shrink, and configuration changes.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 100 dte.event_time, dte.event_name, dte.database_name, dte.login_name, dte.host_name, dte.application_name, dte.object_name, dte.text_data FROM collect.default_trace_events AS dte WHERE dte.event_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) ORDER BY dte.event_time DESC

panels.title: System Health Events
panels.description: System health extended event session data showing deadlocks, memory issues, and scheduler problems.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 50 hpsh.collection_time, hpsh.event_time, hpsh.state, hpsh.systemCpuUtilization, hpsh.sqlCpuUtilization, hpsh.spinlockBackoffs, hpsh.latchWarnings, hpsh.nonYieldingTasksReported FROM collect.HealthParser_SystemHealth AS hpsh WHERE hpsh.collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) ORDER BY hpsh.collection_time DESC

panels.title: Severe Errors
panels.description: Severe SQL Server errors (severity 17+) from system health session and error log.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 50 hpse.collection_time, hpse.event_time, hpse.error_number, hpse.severity, hpse.state, hpse.message, hpse.database_name FROM collect.HealthParser_SevereErrors AS hpse WHERE hpse.collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) ORDER BY hpse.collection_time DESC

panels.title: Configuration Changes
panels.description: SQL Server configuration changes tracked from default trace and system health events.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 50 scc.change_time, scc.configuration_name, scc.old_value_configured, scc.new_value_configured, scc.requires_restart, scc.change_description FROM report.server_configuration_changes AS scc WHERE scc.change_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) ORDER BY scc.change_time DESC

panels.title: Expensive Queries
panels.description: Most expensive queries by CPU, duration, reads, or writes from plan cache and extended events.

query.sqlServer:
SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; 
SELECT TOP 20 source, database_name, object_name, execution_count, avg_worker_time_ms, avg_elapsed_time_ms, avg_logical_reads, query_text_sample , query_plan_xml
FROM report.expensive_queries_today WHERE last_execution_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) 
ORDER BY avg_worker_time_ms DESC

panels.title: Query Statistics
panels.description: Detailed query execution statistics including avg/min/max duration, CPU, and I/O metrics.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 50 database_name, object_name, execution_count, avg_worker_time_ms, avg_elapsed_time_ms, avg_logical_reads, avg_physical_reads, first_execution_time, last_execution_time FROM (SELECT database_name, CASE object_type WHEN 'STATEMENT' THEN 'Adhoc' ELSE QUOTENAME(schema_name) + '.' + QUOTENAME(object_name) END AS object_name, SUM(execution_count) AS execution_count, SUM(total_worker_time) / 1000.0 / NULLIF(SUM(execution_count), 0) AS avg_worker_time_ms, SUM(total_elapsed_time) / 1000.0 / NULLIF(SUM(execution_count), 0) AS avg_elapsed_time_ms, SUM(total_logical_reads) / NULLIF(SUM(execution_count), 0) AS avg_logical_reads, SUM(total_physical_reads) / NULLIF(SUM(execution_count), 0) AS avg_physical_reads, MIN(creation_time) AS first_execution_time, MAX(last_execution_time) AS last_execution_time FROM collect.query_stats WHERE last_execution_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) GROUP BY database_name, query_hash, object_type, schema_name, object_name) q ORDER BY avg_worker_time_ms DESC

panels.title: Blocking Events
panels.description: Blocking events captured from extended events showing blocker/blocked session pairs and wait times.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 50 collection_time, event_time, database_name, activity, spid, wait_time_ms, status, isolation_level, lock_mode, login_name, host_name, client_app FROM collect.blocking_BlockedProcessReport WHERE collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) ORDER BY event_time DESC

panels.title: Query Execution Trends
panels.description: Query execution trends over time showing execution count and performance metric changes.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; WITH exec_deltas AS (SELECT qs.collection_time, total_execution_count = SUM(qs.execution_count_delta), interval_seconds = DATEDIFF(SECOND, LAG(qs.collection_time, 1, qs.collection_time) OVER (ORDER BY qs.collection_time), qs.collection_time) FROM collect.query_stats AS qs WHERE qs.collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) GROUP BY qs.collection_time) SELECT ed.collection_time AS [Time], 'Executions/sec' AS [Series], CAST(ed.total_execution_count AS decimal(19, 4)) / ed.interval_seconds AS [Value] FROM exec_deltas AS ed WHERE ed.interval_seconds > 0 ORDER BY ed.collection_time

panels.title: Memory Statistics
panels.description: Comprehensive memory statistics including total, available, and committed memory from sys.dm_os_sys_memory.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT ms.collection_time AS [Time], 'Buffer Pool MB' AS [Series], ms.buffer_pool_mb AS [Value] FROM collect.memory_stats AS ms WHERE ms.collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) UNION ALL SELECT ms.collection_time, 'Plan Cache MB', ms.plan_cache_mb FROM collect.memory_stats AS ms WHERE ms.collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) UNION ALL SELECT ms.collection_time, 'Other Memory MB', ms.other_memory_mb FROM collect.memory_stats AS ms WHERE ms.collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) ORDER BY [Time]

panels.title: Memory Clerks
panels.description: Detailed memory clerk allocation showing top memory consumers by clerk type.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 20 mcs.collection_time, mcs.clerk_type, mcs.pages_kb / 1024 AS pages_mb, CASE WHEN mcs.clerk_type = 'MEMORYCLERK_SQLBUFFERPOOL' THEN 'Data and index pages cache (Buffer Pool)' WHEN mcs.clerk_type = 'MEMORYCLERK_SQLGENERAL' THEN 'Multiple consumers: replication, diagnostics, parser' WHEN mcs.clerk_type = 'MEMORYCLERK_SQLQUERYEXEC' THEN 'Batch mode, parallel query, sort and hash operations' WHEN mcs.clerk_type = 'CACHESTORE_SQLCP' THEN 'Ad hoc queries and prepared statements in plan cache' WHEN mcs.clerk_type = 'CACHESTORE_OBJCP' THEN 'Stored procedures, functions, triggers in plan cache' ELSE 'See documentation for clerk type details' END AS description FROM collect.memory_clerks_stats AS mcs WHERE mcs.collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -1, SYSDATETIME()) ORDER BY mcs.pages_kb DESC

panels.title: Memory Grant Statistics
panels.description: Active and pending memory grants with grant sizes, wait times, and requesting queries.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT mgs.collection_time AS [Time], 'Granted Memory MB' AS [Series], mgs.granted_memory_mb AS [Value] FROM collect.memory_grant_stats AS mgs WHERE mgs.collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) UNION ALL SELECT mgs.collection_time, 'Available Memory MB', mgs.available_memory_mb FROM collect.memory_grant_stats AS mgs WHERE mgs.collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, SYSDATETIME()) ORDER BY [Time]

panels.title: Plan Cache Statistics
panels.description: Plan cache memory usage by cache type showing size, entry count, and memory consumption.

query.sqlServer: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL READ UNCOMMITTED; SELECT TOP 10 pcs.collection_time, pcs.cacheobjtype, pcs.objtype, pcs.total_plans, pcs.total_size_mb, pcs.single_use_plans, pcs.single_use_size_mb, CASE WHEN pcs.total_plans > 0 AND pcs.single_use_plans * 100.0 / pcs.total_plans > 50 THEN 'CRITICAL' WHEN pcs.total_plans > 0 AND pcs.single_use_plans * 100.0 / pcs.total_plans > 30 THEN 'HIGH' WHEN pcs.total_plans > 0 AND pcs.single_use_plans * 100.0 / pcs.total_plans > 20 THEN 'MEDIUM' ELSE 'NORMAL' END AS bloat_level FROM collect.plan_cache_stats AS pcs WHERE pcs.collection_time >= DATEADD(HOUR, -1, SYSDATETIME()) ORDER BY pcs.total_size_mb DESC

panels.title: CPU Waits
panels.description: CPU-related waits (scheduler yield, parallelism, threadpool). High values indicate CPU saturation.

query.sqlServer: WITH w AS (SELECT wait_type,wait_time_ms FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0) SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN wait_type IN (N'SOS_SCHEDULER_YIELD',N'CXPACKET',N'CXCONSUMER',N'THREADPOOL',N'CMEMTHREAD',N'EXECSYNC',N'SOS_WORK_DISPATCHER',N'WORKER_BOUND') THEN wait_time_ms ELSE 0 END)*100.0/NULLIF(SUM(wait_time_ms),0) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM w

panels.title: I/O Waits
panels.description: I/O waits (page reads, log writes). High values indicate disk bottleneck.

query.sqlServer: WITH w AS (SELECT wait_type,wait_time_ms FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0) SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN wait_type LIKE N'PAGEIOLATCH_%' OR wait_type LIKE N'WRITELOG%' OR wait_type IN (N'ASYNC_IO_COMPLETION',N'IO_COMPLETION',N'BACKUPIO',N'BACKUPTHREAD',N'BACKUPBUFFER',N'IO_QUEUE_LIMIT') THEN wait_time_ms ELSE 0 END)*100.0/NULLIF(SUM(wait_time_ms),0) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM w

panels.title: Memory Waits
panels.description: Memory grant and buffer pool waits. High values indicate memory contention.

query.sqlServer: WITH w AS (SELECT wait_type,wait_time_ms FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0) SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN wait_type IN (N'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE',N'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE_QUERY_COMPILE',N'CMEMPARTITIONED',N'EE_PMOLIST',N'MEMORYBROKER_FOR_CACHE') THEN wait_time_ms ELSE 0 END)*100.0/NULLIF(SUM(wait_time_ms),0) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM w

panels.title: Other Waits
panels.description: All waits not categorised as CPU, I/O, Memory, or Locking.

query.sqlServer: WITH w AS (SELECT wait_type,wait_time_ms FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0) SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN NOT(wait_type IN (N'SOS_SCHEDULER_YIELD',N'CXPACKET',N'CXCONSUMER',N'THREADPOOL',N'CMEMTHREAD',N'EXECSYNC',N'SOS_WORK_DISPATCHER',N'WORKER_BOUND') OR wait_type LIKE N'PAGEIOLATCH_%' OR wait_type LIKE N'WRITELOG%' OR wait_type IN (N'ASYNC_IO_COMPLETION',N'IO_COMPLETION',N'BACKUPIO',N'BACKUPTHREAD',N'BACKUPBUFFER',N'IO_QUEUE_LIMIT') OR wait_type IN (N'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE',N'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE_QUERY_COMPILE',N'CMEMPARTITIONED',N'EE_PMOLIST',N'MEMORYBROKER_FOR_CACHE') OR wait_type LIKE N'LCK_%' OR wait_type LIKE N'LATCH_%' OR wait_type LIKE N'PAGELATCH_%') THEN wait_time_ms ELSE 0 END)*100.0/NULLIF(SUM(wait_time_ms),0) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM w

panels.title: Cumulative Wait
panels.description: Total accumulated wait time in millions of ms since last SQL Server restart. Benign idle waits excluded.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(SUM(wait_time_ms)/1000000.0 AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0

panels.title: Signal Wait %
panels.description: Signal wait as % of total wait. Values above 25% indicate CPU pressure â€” queries spending time in the run queue.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(SUM(signal_wait_time_ms)*100.0/NULLIF(SUM(wait_time_ms),0) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0

panels.title: Unique Wait Types
panels.description: Distinct non-idle wait types recorded since last restart.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT wait_type) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0

panels.title: Active Waits
panels.description: Requests currently waiting in sys.dm_exec_requests. Background and sleeping sessions excluded.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_exec_requests WHERE wait_type IS NOT NULL AND wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'WAITFOR',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH') AND status NOT IN (N'background',N'sleeping')

panels.title: Top Wait Types by Total Wait Time
panels.description: Top 15 wait types by accumulated wait time since restart. Benign idle waits filtered.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 15 wait_type AS [Label], CAST(wait_time_ms AS FLOAT) AS [Value], N'ms' AS [Unit], N'' AS [Instance] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0 ORDER BY wait_time_ms DESC

panels.title: Wait Category Distribution
panels.description: Proportion of cumulative wait time by category. CPU, I/O, Memory, Locking, Other.

query.sqlServer: SELECT cat AS [Label], CAST(SUM(wms)*100.0/NULLIF(SUM(SUM(wms)) OVER(),0) AS FLOAT) AS [Value], N'%' AS [Unit], N'' AS [Instance] FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN wait_type IN (N'SOS_SCHEDULER_YIELD',N'CXPACKET',N'CXCONSUMER',N'THREADPOOL',N'CMEMTHREAD',N'EXECSYNC',N'SOS_WORK_DISPATCHER',N'WORKER_BOUND') THEN N'CPU' WHEN wait_type LIKE N'PAGEIOLATCH_%' OR wait_type LIKE N'WRITELOG%' OR wait_type IN (N'ASYNC_IO_COMPLETION',N'IO_COMPLETION',N'BACKUPIO',N'BACKUPTHREAD',N'BACKUPBUFFER',N'IO_QUEUE_LIMIT') THEN N'I/O' WHEN wait_type IN (N'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE',N'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE_QUERY_COMPILE',N'CMEMPARTITIONED',N'EE_PMOLIST',N'MEMORYBROKER_FOR_CACHE') THEN N'Memory' WHEN wait_type LIKE N'LCK_%' OR wait_type LIKE N'LATCH_%' OR wait_type LIKE N'PAGELATCH_%' THEN N'Locking' ELSE N'Other' END AS cat, wait_time_ms AS wms FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0) x GROUP BY cat ORDER BY [Value] DESC

panels.title: Average Wait Time per Task (ms)
panels.description: Top 15 wait types by average wait per task. High values indicate individual tasks experience significant delays.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 15 wait_type AS [Label], CAST(CASE WHEN waiting_tasks_count>0 THEN wait_time_ms*1.0/waiting_tasks_count ELSE 0 END AS FLOAT) AS [Value], N'ms' AS [Unit], N'' AS [Instance] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0 AND waiting_tasks_count>0 ORDER BY [Value] DESC

panels.title: Signal vs Resource Wait - Top 12
panels.description: Signal wait (CPU queue time) vs resource wait for top wait types. High signal % = CPU pressure.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 12 wait_type AS [Wait Type], CAST(wait_time_ms-signal_wait_time_ms AS BIGINT) AS [Resource Wait ms], CAST(signal_wait_time_ms AS BIGINT) AS [Signal Wait ms], CAST(signal_wait_time_ms*100.0/NULLIF(wait_time_ms,0) AS DECIMAL(5,1)) AS [Signal %], CAST(waiting_tasks_count AS BIGINT) AS [Tasks] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms > 0 ORDER BY wait_time_ms DESC

panels.title: Active Wait Sessions
panels.description: Currently waiting SQL requests - wait type, duration, database and SQL text from sys.dm_exec_requests.

query.sqlServer: SELECT r.session_id AS [SPID], r.wait_type AS [Wait Type], r.wait_time AS [Wait ms], r.status AS [Status], DB_NAME(r.database_id) AS [Database], CAST(SUBSTRING(t.text,(r.statement_start_offset/2)+1,(CASE r.statement_end_offset WHEN -1 THEN DATALENGTH(t.text) ELSE r.statement_end_offset END-r.statement_start_offset)/2+1) AS NVARCHAR(200)) AS [SQL Text] FROM sys.dm_exec_requests r CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(r.sql_handle) t WHERE r.wait_type IS NOT NULL AND r.wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'WAITFOR',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH') AND r.status NOT IN (N'background',N'sleeping') ORDER BY r.wait_time DESC

panels.title: Top 10 Wait Types by Avg Wait (ms)
panels.description: Top 10 wait types ranked by average wait time per task since last SQL Server restart. High avg wait on LCK_M_* = blocking; PAGEIOLATCH_* = I/O; RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE = memory pressure.
