query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 10 wait_type AS [Wait Type], CAST(waiting_tasks_count AS BIGINT) AS [Tasks], CAST(wait_time_ms AS BIGINT) AS [Total Wait ms], CAST(CASE WHEN waiting_tasks_count>0 THEN wait_time_ms*1.0/waiting_tasks_count ELSE 0 END AS DECIMAL(12,2)) AS [Avg Wait ms], CAST(signal_wait_time_ms AS BIGINT) AS [Signal Wait ms], CAST(wait_time_ms-signal_wait_time_ms AS BIGINT) AS [Resource Wait ms], CAST(signal_wait_time_ms*100.0/NULLIF(wait_time_ms,0) AS DECIMAL(5,1)) AS [Signal %] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms>0 AND waiting_tasks_count>0 ORDER BY [Avg Wait ms] DESC

panels.title: Top 10 Wait Types by Total Wait (ms)
panels.description: Top 10 wait types by cumulative wait time since last SQL Server restart. Shows what SQL Server has spent the most time waiting on overall.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 10 wait_type AS [Wait Type], CAST(waiting_tasks_count AS BIGINT) AS [Tasks], CAST(wait_time_ms AS BIGINT) AS [Total Wait ms], CAST(CASE WHEN waiting_tasks_count>0 THEN wait_time_ms*1.0/waiting_tasks_count ELSE 0 END AS DECIMAL(12,2)) AS [Avg Wait ms], CAST(CASE WHEN wait_type IN (N'SOS_SCHEDULER_YIELD',N'CXPACKET',N'CXCONSUMER',N'THREADPOOL',N'CMEMTHREAD',N'EXECSYNC',N'SOS_WORK_DISPATCHER') THEN N'CPU' WHEN wait_type LIKE N'PAGEIOLATCH_%' OR wait_type LIKE N'WRITELOG%' OR wait_type IN (N'ASYNC_IO_COMPLETION',N'IO_COMPLETION',N'BACKUPIO') THEN N'I/O' WHEN wait_type IN (N'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE',N'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE_QUERY_COMPILE',N'CMEMPARTITIONED') THEN N'Memory' WHEN wait_type LIKE N'LCK_%' OR wait_type LIKE N'LATCH_%' OR wait_type LIKE N'PAGELATCH_%' THEN N'Locking' ELSE N'Other' END AS NVARCHAR(20)) AS [Category] FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WHERE wait_type NOT IN (N'SLEEP_TASK',N'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK',N'SLEEP_DBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_DCOMSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_MASTERDBREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERMDREADY',N'SLEEP_MASTERUPGRADED',N'SLEEP_MSDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSTARTUP',N'SLEEP_TEMPDBSHRINK',N'SLEEP_WORKSPACE_MANAGER',N'SLEEP_DIAG',N'SLEEP_WORKER',N'BROKER_TO_FLUSH',N'BROKER_EVENTHANDLER',N'BROKER_TRANSMITTER',N'CHECKPOINT_QUEUE',N'DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',N'DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',N'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT',N'HADR_WORK_QUEUE',N'HADR_LOGCAPTURE_WAIT',N'LAZYWRITER_SLEEP',N'LOGMGR_QUEUE',N'ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE',N'REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOAD_SEARCH',N'RESOURCE_QUEUE',N'SERVER_IDLE_CHECK',N'SNI_HTTP_ACCEPT',N'SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',N'SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH',N'SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP',N'WAITFOR',N'XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT',N'XE_TIMER_EVENT',N'CLR_AUTO_EVENT',N'CLR_MANUAL_EVENT',N'DBMIRROR_WORKER_QUEUE',N'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG',N'XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN') AND wait_time_ms>0 ORDER BY wait_time_ms DESC

panels.title: Missing Index Count
panels.description: Total missing indexes detected by the query optimizer since last restart.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_db_missing_index_details

panels.title: High Impact Missing Indexes
panels.description: Top missing indexes ranked by optimizer impact score (seeks × cost × impact). Create these first.

query.sqlServer:
DECLARE @c_r AS CHAR(2) ;
SET @c_r = CHAR(13) + CHAR(10);

DECLARE @SampleSizeRows NVARCHAR(25)
SET @SampleSizeRows = '50000'
	SELECT TOP 50
				T1.DB [Database]
				,T1.[Table]
				,ISNULL(T1.equality_columns,'') [Equality Cols]
				,ISNULL(T1.inequality_columns,'') [Inequality Cols]
				,ISNULL(T1.included_columns,'') [Include Cols]
				,CONVERT(VARCHAR,T1.last_user_seek,120) [Last Seek]
				,CONVERT(VARCHAR,T1.last_user_scan,120) [Last Scan]
				,T1.[Cost]
				,T1.[Cost Saving %]
				, T1.[Seeks]
				, T1.[Scans]
				, T1.[Impact Score]
			,'SELECT '''+ REPLACE(T1.[CreateIndexStatement],'< be clever here >', ' ''+ ('+  replace(CONVERT([NVARCHAR] (4000),BeingClever  ), '  ',' ') + ') + '' ') + ''' ' [Create Script]
			FROM (		
			SELECT 
				DB_NAME(database_id) [DB] --LEFT([statement],(PATINDEX('%.%',[statement]))-1) 
				,  (( user_seeks + user_scans ) * avg_total_user_cost * avg_user_impact) magic_benefit_number 
				,migs.last_user_seek	
				,migs.last_user_scan
				,CONVERT(MONEY,migs.avg_total_user_cost) [Cost]
				,migs.avg_user_impact [Cost Saving %]
				, CAST(migs.avg_total_user_cost * migs.avg_user_impact * (migs.user_seeks + migs.user_scans) AS DECIMAL(18,0)) AS [Impact Score]
				, migs.user_seeks AS [Seeks]
				, migs.user_scans AS [Scans]
				, [Table] = [statement]
				, [CreateIndexStatement] = 'CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_SQLDBA_' + REPLACE(CONVERT([VARCHAR](20),GETDATE(),102),'.','_') + 'T'  + REPLACE(CONVERT([VARCHAR](20),GETDATE(),108),':','_') + '_'
				+ REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(LEFT(ISNULL(mid.equality_columns,'')+ISNULL(mid.inequality_columns,''),35), '[', ''), ']',''), ', ','_')  + ' ON ' + [statement] + @c_r
				+  ' ( ' + @c_r
				+ '< be clever here > ' + @c_r
				+  ') ' + @c_r
				+  ISNULL('INCLUDE (' + mid.included_columns + ')','') + @c_r
				+ 'WITH (SORT_IN_TEMPDB = ON, DATA_COMPRESSION=PAGE);'
				, mid.equality_columns
				, mid.inequality_columns
				, mid.included_columns
				, N'SELECT STUFF(( SELECT '', '' + [Columns] ' + @c_r + '
			FROM ( SELECT TOP 100 PERCENT c1.[id], [Columns], [Count] ' + @c_r + '
			FROM ( ' + @c_r + '
			SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY [RankMe]) [id], LTRIM([Columns]) [Columns]  ' + @c_r + '
			FROM (VALUES(''' + REPLACE(ISNULL(mid.equality_columns + ISNULL(','+ mid.inequality_columns,''),ISNULL(mid.inequality_columns,'')) ,',',''',1),(''') 
			+''',1))t ([Columns],[RankMe]) ) c1 ' + @c_r 
			+ ' LEFT OUTER JOIN ( ' + @c_r + '
			SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER(ORDER BY [Count]) [id] ,LTRIM([Count]) [Count] ' + @c_r + '
			FROM (VALUES((
			SELECT TOP '+@SampleSizeRows+' 
			COUNT (DISTINCT ' + REPLACE(ISNULL(mid.equality_columns + ISNULL(','+ mid.inequality_columns,''),ISNULL(mid.inequality_columns,'')) ,','
			,') FROM ' + [statement] +' WITH (NOLOCK)))
			,((SELECT TOP '+@SampleSizeRows+' COUNT (DISTINCT ')  + @c_r 
			+') FROM ' + [statement] +' WITH (NOLOCK))))t ([Count]) )c2 ON c2.id = c1.id  ' + @c_r + '
			ORDER BY c2.[Count] * 1 DESC ' + @c_r + '
			) t1 FOR XML PATH('''')),1,1,'''') AS NameValues' [BeingClever]

			FROM [sys].dm_db_missing_index_group_stats AS migs 
			INNER JOIN [sys].dm_db_missing_index_groups AS mig ON migs.group_handle = mig.index_group_handle 
			INNER JOIN [sys].dm_db_missing_index_details AS mid ON mig.index_handle = mid.index_handle 

			)T1 

			WHERE T1.magic_benefit_number > 500
			
			ORDER BY [Database],[Table], magic_benefit_number DESC 
			
			OPTION (RECOMPILE)

panels.title: Index Fragmentation (>30%)
panels.description: Indexes with over 30% fragmentation and more than 100 pages. Candidates for REBUILD or REORGANIZE.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 30 DB_NAME(s.database_id) AS [Database], OBJECT_NAME(s.object_id,s.database_id) AS [Table], i.name AS [Index], s.index_type_desc AS [Type], CAST(s.avg_fragmentation_in_percent AS DECIMAL(5,1)) AS [Frag %], s.page_count AS [Pages] FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(NULL,NULL,NULL,NULL,N'LIMITED') s JOIN sys.indexes i ON s.object_id = i.object_id AND s.index_id = i.index_id WHERE s.avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 30 AND s.page_count > 100 AND i.name IS NOT NULL ORDER BY s.avg_fragmentation_in_percent DESC

panels.title: Unused Indexes (Write-Heavy)
panels.description: Indexes with zero reads but active writes since last restart. Candidates for removal to reduce write overhead.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 20 DB_NAME(u.database_id) AS [Database], OBJECT_NAME(u.object_id,u.database_id) AS [Table], i.name AS [Index], u.user_seeks AS [Seeks], u.user_scans AS [Scans], u.user_lookups AS [Lookups], u.user_updates AS [Writes] FROM sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats u JOIN sys.indexes i ON u.object_id = i.object_id AND u.index_id = i.index_id WHERE u.user_seeks = 0 AND u.user_scans = 0 AND u.user_lookups = 0 AND i.name IS NOT NULL AND i.index_id > 1 AND u.user_updates > 0 ORDER BY u.user_updates DESC

panels.title: DBs Without Full Backup (7d)
panels.description: Databases with no full backup in the last 7 days.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0 AND d.name NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT database_name FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE type = 'D' AND backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()))

panels.title: DBs Without Log Backup (1h)
panels.description: FULL recovery model databases with no transaction log backup in the last hour.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.recovery_model = 1 AND d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0 AND d.name NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT database_name FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE type = 'L' AND backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(HOUR,-1,GETDATE()))

panels.title: Last Full Backup per Database
panels.description: Most recent full backup date, age in hours and size for each user database.

query.sqlServer: SELECT d.name AS [Database], d.recovery_model_desc AS [Recovery Model], MAX(b.backup_finish_date) AS [Last Full Backup], DATEDIFF(HOUR,MAX(b.backup_finish_date),GETDATE()) AS [Hours Ago], CAST(MAX(b.backup_size)/1024.0/1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Size MB] FROM sys.databases d LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset b ON b.database_name = d.name AND b.type = 'D' WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0 GROUP BY d.name,d.recovery_model_desc ORDER BY [Last Full Backup] ASC

panels.title: Recent Backup History
panels.description: Last 50 backup operations from msdb, newest first.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 50 bs.database_name AS [Database], CASE bs.type WHEN 'D' THEN 'Full' WHEN 'I' THEN 'Differential' WHEN 'L' THEN 'Log' ELSE bs.type END AS [Type], bs.backup_start_date AS [Started], bs.backup_finish_date AS [Finished], DATEDIFF(MINUTE,bs.backup_start_date,bs.backup_finish_date) AS [Duration (min)], CAST(bs.backup_size/1024.0/1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Size MB], bs.user_name AS [By] FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs ORDER BY bs.backup_finish_date DESC

panels.title: Backups Without Checksum
panels.description: Databases whose most recent full backup was taken without checksum verification. Backups without checksum cannot detect media corruption and cannot be used for RESTORE WITH CHECKSUM validation.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM (SELECT bs.database_name, MAX(bs.backup_finish_date) AS last_backup, MAX(CASE WHEN bs.has_backup_checksums = 1 THEN bs.backup_finish_date END) AS last_checksum_backup FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs WHERE bs.type = 'D' AND bs.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) GROUP BY bs.database_name) x WHERE last_checksum_backup IS NULL OR last_backup > last_checksum_backup

panels.title: Backups Without Compression
panels.description: Databases whose most recent full backup was taken without compression. Uncompressed backups consume significantly more storage and take longer to restore.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM (SELECT bs.database_name, MAX(bs.backup_finish_date) AS last_backup FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs WHERE bs.type = 'D' AND bs.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) AND ISNULL(bs.compressed_backup_size, 0) >= bs.backup_size GROUP BY bs.database_name) x

panels.title: Last Restore per Database
panels.description: Most recent successful RESTORE operation per database. A NULL [Last Restored] means the database has never been test-restored — a significant RPO risk. Includes restore type, duration, and who performed it.

query.sqlServer: SELECT d.name AS [Database], d.recovery_model_desc AS [Recovery], MAX(rh.restore_date) AS [Last Restored], DATEDIFF(DAY,MAX(rh.restore_date),GETDATE()) AS [Days Since Restore], MAX(rh.user_name) AS [Restored By], MAX(CASE WHEN rh.restore_type = 'D' THEN 'Full' WHEN rh.restore_type = 'I' THEN 'Differential' WHEN rh.restore_type = 'L' THEN 'Log' WHEN rh.restore_type = 'V' THEN 'Verifyonly' ELSE rh.restore_type END) AS [Type] FROM sys.databases d LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.restorehistory rh ON rh.destination_database_name = d.name WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0 GROUP BY d.name, d.recovery_model_desc ORDER BY MAX(rh.restore_date) ASC

panels.title: Checksum & Compression Detail
panels.description: Per-database breakdown of whether the most recent full backup used checksum and compression. Sorted by risk: no checksum and no compression first.

query.sqlServer: SELECT bs.database_name AS [Database], MAX(bs.backup_finish_date) AS [Last Full Backup], CASE MAX(CAST(bs.has_backup_checksums AS INT)) WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS [Checksum], CASE WHEN MAX(bs.compressed_backup_size) < MAX(bs.backup_size) THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS [Compressed], CAST(MAX(bs.backup_size)/1024.0/1024.0/1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS [Raw Size GB], CAST(MAX(bs.compressed_backup_size)/1024.0/1024.0/1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS [Compressed Size GB] FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs WHERE bs.type = 'D' AND bs.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) GROUP BY bs.database_name ORDER BY MAX(CAST(bs.has_backup_checksums AS INT)) ASC, bs.database_name

dashboards.title: Query Store Health
dashboards.description: Per-database Query Store operational state, storage consumption, forced plans, and regressed queries. Requires SQL Server 2016+ with Query Store enabled.

panels.title: Databases with Query Store Not Healthy
panels.description: Databases where Query Store is in ERROR or READ_ONLY state (usually means it ran out of space). IQP features silently stop working when QS is not READ_WRITE.

query.sqlServer: DECLARE @sql NVARCHAR(MAX) = N''; SELECT @sql = @sql + N'SELECT ' + QUOTENAME(name, '''') + N' AS db FROM ' + QUOTENAME(name) + N'.sys.database_query_store_options WHERE actual_state_desc <> N''READ_WRITE'' UNION ALL ' FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = N'ONLINE' AND is_query_store_on = 1; IF LEN(@sql) > 0 BEGIN SET @sql = LEFT(@sql, LEN(@sql) - 10); SET @sql = N'SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM (' + @sql + N') x'; EXEC sp_executesql @sql; END ELSE SELECT CAST(0 AS FLOAT) AS [Value];

panels.title: Databases with Query Store Disabled
panels.description: User databases that do not have Query Store enabled. IQP persistence (MGF, DOP, CE feedback) requires Query Store in READ_WRITE mode.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.databases WHERE is_query_store_on = 0 AND database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE'

panels.title: Forced Plans Active
panels.description: Count of query plans currently forced via Query Store. Forced plans are emergency fixes — they should be reviewed regularly as they may become stale.

query.sqlServer: DECLARE @sql NVARCHAR(MAX)=''; SELECT @sql=@sql+'SELECT '+QUOTENAME(name,'''')+' AS db, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM ['+name+'].sys.query_store_plan WHERE is_forced_plan=1 UNION ALL ' FROM sys.databases WHERE is_query_store_on=1 AND state_desc='ONLINE' AND database_id>4; IF LEN(@sql)>0 BEGIN SET @sql=LEFT(@sql,LEN(@sql)-10); DECLARE @t TABLE(db NVARCHAR(128),cnt INT); INSERT @t EXEC(@sql); SELECT CAST(SUM(cnt) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM @t; END ELSE SELECT CAST(0 AS FLOAT) AS [Value]

panels.title: Query Store Status Per Database
panels.description: Per-database Query Store state, storage used vs. cap, stale query threshold, and capture mode. Highlight databases approaching storage limit.

query.sqlServer: DECLARE @sql NVARCHAR(MAX) = N''; SELECT @sql = @sql + N'SELECT ' + QUOTENAME(name, '''') + N' AS [Database], o.actual_state_desc AS [State], o.current_storage_size_mb AS [Used MB], o.max_storage_size_mb AS [Max MB], CAST(o.current_storage_size_mb*100.0/NULLIF(o.max_storage_size_mb,0) AS DECIMAL(5,1)) AS [Used %], o.query_capture_mode_desc AS [Capture Mode], o.stale_query_threshold_days AS [Stale Threshold (days)] FROM ' + QUOTENAME(name) + N'.sys.database_query_store_options o UNION ALL ' FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = N'ONLINE' AND is_query_store_on = 1; IF LEN(@sql) > 0 BEGIN SET @sql = LEFT(@sql, LEN(@sql) - 10); SET @sql = N'SELECT * FROM (' + @sql + N') x ORDER BY [Used %] DESC'; EXEC sp_executesql @sql; END ELSE SELECT CAST(NULL AS NVARCHAR(128)) AS [Database], N'N/A' AS [State], 0 AS [Used MB], 0 AS [Max MB], CAST(0 AS DECIMAL(5,1)) AS [Used %], N'N/A' AS [Capture Mode], 0 AS [Stale Threshold (days)] WHERE 1=0;

panels.title: Forced Plans Detail
panels.description: All currently forced plans across all databases. Shows database, query text, plan creation time and when forcing was applied.

query.sqlServer: DECLARE @sql NVARCHAR(MAX)=''; SELECT @sql=@sql+'SELECT '+QUOTENAME(name,'''')+' AS [Database], qt.query_sql_text AS [SQL Text], p.plan_id AS [Plan ID], p.last_compile_start_time AS [Last Compiled], p.force_failure_count AS [Force Failures] FROM ['+name+'].sys.query_store_plan p JOIN ['+name+'].sys.query_store_query q ON p.query_id=q.query_id JOIN ['+name+'].sys.query_store_query_text qt ON q.query_text_id=qt.query_text_id WHERE p.is_forced_plan=1 UNION ALL ' FROM sys.databases WHERE is_query_store_on=1 AND state_desc='ONLINE' AND database_id>4; IF LEN(@sql)>0 BEGIN SET @sql=LEFT(@sql,LEN(@sql)-10)+' ORDER BY [Database],[Last Compiled] DESC'; EXEC(@sql); END

panels.title: Recently Regressed Queries
panels.description: Queries where average duration in the last 24 hours is more than 2x the historical average. Based on Query Store runtime stats across all enabled databases.

query.sqlServer: DECLARE @sql NVARCHAR(MAX)=''; SELECT @sql=@sql+'SELECT TOP 20 '+QUOTENAME(name,'''')+' AS [Database], qt.query_sql_text AS [SQL Text], CAST(recent.avg_duration/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(12,2)) AS [Recent Avg ms], CAST(hist.avg_duration/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(12,2)) AS [Historical Avg ms], CAST(recent.avg_duration*1.0/NULLIF(hist.avg_duration,0) AS DECIMAL(6,1)) AS [Regression Factor], recent.count_executions AS [Recent Executions] FROM (SELECT q.query_id, qt.query_text_id, qt.query_sql_text, AVG(rs.avg_duration) avg_duration, SUM(rs.count_executions) count_executions FROM ['+name+'].sys.query_store_query q JOIN ['+name+'].sys.query_store_query_text qt ON q.query_text_id=qt.query_text_id JOIN ['+name+'].sys.query_store_plan p ON p.query_id=q.query_id JOIN ['+name+'].sys.query_store_runtime_stats rs ON rs.plan_id=p.plan_id JOIN ['+name+'].sys.query_store_runtime_stats_interval i ON i.runtime_stats_interval_id=rs.runtime_stats_interval_id WHERE i.start_time>=DATEADD(HOUR,-24,GETDATE()) GROUP BY q.query_id,qt.query_text_id,qt.query_sql_text) recent JOIN ['+name+'].sys.query_store_query_text qt ON qt.query_text_id=recent.query_text_id JOIN (SELECT q.query_id, AVG(rs.avg_duration) avg_duration FROM ['+name+'].sys.query_store_query q JOIN ['+name+'].sys.query_store_plan p ON p.query_id=q.query_id JOIN ['+name+'].sys.query_store_runtime_stats rs ON rs.plan_id=p.plan_id JOIN ['+name+'].sys.query_store_runtime_stats_interval i ON i.runtime_stats_interval_id=rs.runtime_stats_interval_id WHERE i.start_time BETWEEN DATEADD(DAY,-7,GETDATE()) AND DATEADD(DAY,-1,GETDATE()) GROUP BY q.query_id) hist ON hist.query_id=recent.query_id WHERE recent.avg_duration>hist.avg_duration*2 AND hist.avg_duration>10000 UNION ALL ' FROM sys.databases WHERE is_query_store_on=1 AND state_desc='ONLINE' AND database_id>4; IF LEN(@sql)>0 BEGIN SET @sql=LEFT(@sql,LEN(@sql)-10)+' ORDER BY [Regression Factor] DESC'; EXEC(@sql); END

dashboards.title: Database Health
dashboards.description: Per-database security, recovery, IQP configuration, I/O latency, CPU/IO/memory consumption and RPO status. Sourced from live DMVs — no SQLWATCH required.

panels.title: Databases Without TDE
panels.description: User databases that do not have Transparent Data Encryption enabled. Unencrypted databases can be read by attaching the data file without credentials.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE' AND is_encrypted = 0 AND name NOT IN ('master','model','msdb','tempdb')

panels.title: Databases with High VLF Count
panels.description: Databases with more than 200 Virtual Log Files. High VLF counts slow crash recovery and log shipping. Caused by small auto-growth increments.

query.sqlServer: IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#vlf') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #vlf; CREATE TABLE #vlf (db NVARCHAR(128), vlf_count INT); DECLARE @nm NVARCHAR(128), @sql NVARCHAR(500); DECLARE c CURSOR LOCAL FAST_FORWARD FOR SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE state_desc='ONLINE' AND database_id>4; OPEN c; FETCH NEXT FROM c INTO @nm; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS=0 BEGIN SET @sql='INSERT #vlf SELECT '+QUOTENAME(@nm,'''')+', COUNT(*) FROM '+QUOTENAME(@nm)+'.sys.dm_db_log_info(DEFAULT)'; BEGIN TRY EXEC(@sql); END TRY BEGIN CATCH END CATCH; FETCH NEXT FROM c INTO @nm; END; CLOSE c; DEALLOCATE c; SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM #vlf WHERE vlf_count>200

panels.title: SQL Server Audits Stopped
panels.description: SQL Server Audit objects that exist but are not currently running. A stopped audit means security events are not being captured.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.server_audits WHERE is_state_enabled = 0

panels.title: Database Security & Recovery Overview
panels.description: Per-database TDE status, encryption state, recovery model, RCSI, ADR, compatibility level and VLF count. Key configuration baseline for each database.

query.sqlServer: SELECT d.name AS [Database], d.compatibility_level AS [Compat], d.recovery_model_desc AS [Recovery], CASE d.is_encrypted WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS [TDE], ISNULL((SELECT state_desc FROM sys.dm_database_encryption_keys ek WHERE ek.database_id=d.database_id),'N/A') AS [Encrypt State], CASE d.is_read_committed_snapshot_on WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS [RCSI], CASE d.is_accelerated_database_recovery_on WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS [ADR], CASE d.is_query_store_on WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS [QStore], d.page_verify_option_desc AS [Page Verify], d.is_auto_close_on AS [AutoClose], d.is_auto_shrink_on AS [AutoShrink] FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state_desc = 'ONLINE' ORDER BY d.name

panels.title: Database Configuration
panels.description: Per-database configuration flags: compatibility level, recovery model, Legacy CE, QO Hotfixes, auto stats, parameterization, and RCSI. All columns available on SQL Server 2016+.

query.sqlServer: DECLARE @sql NVARCHAR(MAX) = N''; SELECT @sql = @sql + N'SELECT ' + QUOTENAME(name,'''') + N' AS [Database], ' + CAST(compatibility_level AS NVARCHAR(5)) + N' AS [Compat], ' + QUOTENAME(recovery_model_desc,'''') + N' AS [Recovery], CASE (SELECT CAST(value AS INT) FROM ' + QUOTENAME(name) + N'.sys.database_scoped_configurations WHERE name=''LEGACY_CARDINALITY_ESTIMATION'') WHEN 1 THEN ''ON'' ELSE ''off'' END AS [Legacy CE], CASE (SELECT CAST(value AS INT) FROM ' + QUOTENAME(name) + N'.sys.database_scoped_configurations WHERE name=''QUERY_OPTIMIZER_HOTFIXES'') WHEN 1 THEN ''ON'' ELSE ''off'' END AS [QO Hotfixes], CASE ' + CAST(is_auto_create_stats_on AS NVARCHAR(1)) + N' WHEN 1 THEN ''ON'' ELSE ''off'' END AS [Auto Stats Create], CASE ' + CAST(is_auto_update_stats_on AS NVARCHAR(1)) + N' WHEN 1 THEN ''ON'' ELSE ''off'' END AS [Auto Stats Update], CASE ' + CAST(is_parameterization_forced AS NVARCHAR(1)) + N' WHEN 1 THEN ''Forced'' ELSE ''Simple'' END AS [Parameterization], CASE ' + CAST(is_read_committed_snapshot_on AS NVARCHAR(1)) + N' WHEN 1 THEN ''ON'' ELSE ''off'' END AS [RCSI] UNION ALL ' FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = N'ONLINE'; IF LEN(@sql) > 0 BEGIN SET @sql = LEFT(@sql, LEN(@sql)-10) + N' ORDER BY [Database]'; EXEC sp_executesql @sql; END ELSE SELECT CAST(NULL AS NVARCHAR(128)) AS [Database], NULL AS [Compat], NULL AS [Recovery], NULL AS [Legacy CE], NULL AS [QO Hotfixes], NULL AS [Auto Stats Create], NULL AS [Auto Stats Update], NULL AS [Parameterization], NULL AS [RCSI] WHERE 1=0;

panels.title: Database I/O Latency (from DMVs)
panels.description: Per-database read/write latency, total IO operations and daily GB since last SQL Server restart. Based on triage section 10/14. Alert: >10ms warning, >100ms critical.

query.sqlServer: DECLARE @days FLOAT; SELECT @days = DATEDIFF(SECOND,sqlserver_start_time,GETDATE())/86400.0 FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info; SELECT DB_NAME(s.database_id) AS [Database], SUM(s.num_of_reads) AS [Reads], SUM(s.num_of_writes) AS [Writes], SUM(s.num_of_reads+s.num_of_writes) AS [Total IO], CAST(CASE WHEN SUM(s.num_of_reads+s.num_of_writes)=0 THEN 0  ELSE SUM(s.io_stall)*1.0/SUM(s.num_of_reads+s.num_of_writes) END AS DECIMAL(12,2)) AS [Avg Latency ms], CAST(CASE WHEN SUM(s.num_of_reads)=0 THEN 0  ELSE SUM(s.io_stall_read_ms)*1.0/SUM(s.num_of_reads) END AS DECIMAL(12,2)) AS [Read Latency ms], CAST(CASE WHEN SUM(s.num_of_writes)=0 THEN 0  ELSE SUM(s.io_stall_write_ms)*1.0/SUM(s.num_of_writes) END AS DECIMAL(12,2)) AS [Write Latency ms], CAST((SUM(s.num_of_reads+s.num_of_writes))*8.0/1024/1024/NULLIF(@days,0) AS DECIMAL(12,3)) AS [Daily IO GB] FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL,NULL) s JOIN sys.databases d ON d.database_id=s.database_id WHERE d.database_id > 4 GROUP BY s.database_id ORDER BY [Avg Latency ms] DESC

panels.title: Database CPU / IO / Memory Breakdown
panels.description: Per-database share of CPU time (from plan cache), total IO operations, buffer pool pages and disk usage. Based on triage section 14. Shows which databases are driving server resource usage.

query.sqlServer: SELECT io.db_name AS [Database], CAST(ISNULL(cpu.cpu_pct,0) AS DECIMAL(5,1)) AS [CPU %], CAST(io.io_pct AS DECIMAL(5,1)) AS [IO %], CAST(ISNULL(buf.buf_pct,0) AS DECIMAL(5,1)) AS [Buffer %], CAST(ISNULL(buf.buf_mb,0) AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Buffer MB], io.total_io AS [Total IO], io.reads AS [Reads], io.writes AS [Writes], CAST(io.daily_gb AS DECIMAL(12,3)) AS [Daily IO GB], CAST(io.avg_latency_ms AS DECIMAL(12,2)) AS [Avg Latency ms] FROM (SELECT d.name db_name, SUM(s.num_of_reads) reads, SUM(s.num_of_writes) writes, SUM(s.num_of_reads+s.num_of_writes) total_io, CAST(SUM(s.num_of_reads+s.num_of_writes)*100.0/NULLIF(SUM(SUM(s.num_of_reads+s.num_of_writes)) OVER(),0) AS DECIMAL(12,4)) io_pct, CAST((SUM(s.num_of_reads+s.num_of_writes))*8.0/1024/1024/NULLIF((SELECT DATEDIFF(SECOND,sqlserver_start_time,GETDATE())/86400.0 FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info),0) AS DECIMAL(12,3)) daily_gb, CAST(CASE WHEN SUM(s.num_of_reads+s.num_of_writes)=0 THEN 0 ELSE SUM(s.io_stall)*1.0/SUM(s.num_of_reads+s.num_of_writes) END AS DECIMAL(12,2)) avg_latency_ms FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL,NULL) s JOIN sys.databases d ON d.database_id=s.database_id WHERE d.database_id>4 GROUP BY d.name) io LEFT JOIN (SELECT DB_NAME(epa.db_id) db_name, CAST(SUM(qs.total_worker_time)*100.0/NULLIF(SUM(SUM(qs.total_worker_time)) OVER(),0) AS DECIMAL(12,4)) cpu_pct FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs CROSS APPLY (SELECT CONVERT(INT,value) db_id FROM sys.dm_exec_plan_attributes(qs.plan_handle) WHERE attribute='dbid') epa WHERE DB_NAME(epa.db_id) IS NOT NULL GROUP BY DB_NAME(epa.db_id)) cpu ON cpu.db_name=io.db_name LEFT JOIN (SELECT DB_NAME(database_id) db_name, COUNT_BIG(*) buf_pages, CAST(COUNT_BIG(*)/128.0 AS DECIMAL(12,1)) buf_mb, CAST(COUNT_BIG(*)*100.0/NULLIF(SUM(COUNT_BIG(*)) OVER(),0) AS DECIMAL(12,4)) buf_pct FROM sys.dm_os_buffer_descriptors GROUP BY database_id) buf ON buf.db_name=io.db_name ORDER BY io.total_io DESC

panels.title: Database RPO (minutes since last backup)
panels.description: For FULL recovery databases: minutes since last transaction log backup. For SIMPLE: hours since last full backup. Inspired by triage section 9. Alert thresholds: >60 min = warning, >480 min = critical.

query.sqlServer: SELECT d.name AS [Database], d.recovery_model_desc AS [Recovery Model], CAST(ISNULL(MAX(CASE bs.type WHEN 'D' THEN bs.backup_finish_date END),d.create_date) AS DATETIME) AS [Last Full], CAST(MAX(CASE bs.type WHEN 'L' THEN bs.backup_finish_date END) AS DATETIME) AS [Last Log], CASE WHEN d.recovery_model_desc='FULL' THEN DATEDIFF(MINUTE, ISNULL(MAX(CASE bs.type WHEN 'L' THEN bs.backup_finish_date END), ISNULL(MAX(CASE bs.type WHEN 'D' THEN bs.backup_finish_date END),d.create_date)), GETDATE()) ELSE DATEDIFF(MINUTE, ISNULL(MAX(CASE bs.type WHEN 'D' THEN bs.backup_finish_date END),d.create_date), GETDATE()) END AS [RPO (minutes)], CASE WHEN d.recovery_model_desc='FULL' AND MAX(CASE bs.type WHEN 'L' THEN bs.backup_finish_date END) IS NULL THEN 'No log backup found' WHEN d.recovery_model_desc='FULL' AND DATEDIFF(MINUTE,MAX(CASE bs.type WHEN 'L' THEN bs.backup_finish_date END),GETDATE())>480 THEN 'CRITICAL' WHEN d.recovery_model_desc='FULL' AND DATEDIFF(MINUTE,MAX(CASE bs.type WHEN 'L' THEN bs.backup_finish_date END),GETDATE())>60 THEN 'WARNING' ELSE 'OK' END AS [RPO Status], CAST(AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND,bs.backup_start_date,bs.backup_finish_date) AS FLOAT))/60.0 AS DECIMAL(8,1)) AS [Avg Backup Time (min)], CAST(AVG(CAST(bs.compressed_backup_size AS FLOAT))/1024/1024/1024 AS DECIMAL(8,2)) AS [Avg Compressed GB] FROM sys.databases d LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset bs ON bs.database_name=d.name AND bs.backup_finish_date>=DATEADD(DAY,-30,GETDATE()) WHERE d.database_id>4 AND d.state_desc='ONLINE' GROUP BY d.name,d.recovery_model_desc,d.create_date ORDER BY [RPO (minutes)] DESC

panels.title: SQL Server Audit Status
panels.description: All SQL Server Audit objects, their state, target (file/application log), and current audit file path from sys.dm_server_audit_status.

query.sqlServer: SELECT a.name AS [Audit Name], CASE a.is_state_enabled WHEN 1 THEN 'Running' ELSE 'STOPPED' END AS [State], a.type_desc AS [Target], ISNULL(s.audit_file_path,'') AS [Current File], s.audit_file_size AS [File Size (bytes)] FROM sys.server_audits a LEFT JOIN sys.dm_server_audit_status s ON s.audit_id=a.audit_id ORDER BY a.is_state_enabled ASC, a.name

panels.title: VLF Count Per Database
panels.description: Virtual Log File count per database from sys.dm_db_log_info. >200 VLFs slows crash recovery. >1000 VLFs = serious problem. Fix with DBCC SHRINKFILE + controlled log growth.

query.sqlServer: IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#vlf2') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #vlf2; CREATE TABLE #vlf2 (db NVARCHAR(128), vlf_count INT); DECLARE @nm2 NVARCHAR(128), @sql2 NVARCHAR(500); DECLARE c2 CURSOR LOCAL FAST_FORWARD FOR SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE state_desc='ONLINE' AND database_id>4; OPEN c2; FETCH NEXT FROM c2 INTO @nm2; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS=0 BEGIN SET @sql2='INSERT #vlf2 SELECT '+QUOTENAME(@nm2,'''')+', COUNT(*) FROM '+QUOTENAME(@nm2)+'.sys.dm_db_log_info(DEFAULT)'; BEGIN TRY EXEC(@sql2); END TRY BEGIN CATCH END CATCH; FETCH NEXT FROM c2 INTO @nm2; END; CLOSE c2; DEALLOCATE c2; SELECT db AS [Database], vlf_count AS [VLF Count], CASE WHEN vlf_count>1000 THEN 'CRITICAL' WHEN vlf_count>200 THEN 'WARNING' ELSE 'OK' END AS [Status] FROM #vlf2 ORDER BY vlf_count DESC

panels.title: Plan Cache Hit Ratio
panels.description: SQL Server plan cache hit ratio from sys.dm_os_performance_counters.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(cntr_value AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters WHERE counter_name = 'Cache Hit Ratio' AND object_name LIKE '%Plan Cache%' AND instance_name = '_Total'

panels.title: Plans Cached
panels.description: Total compiled plans currently in the SQL Server plan cache.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_exec_cached_plans WHERE cacheobjtype = 'Compiled Plan'

panels.title: Top CPU Queries
panels.description: Top 20 queries by total CPU time since last restart from sys.dm_exec_query_stats.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT TOP 20 CAST(qs.total_worker_time/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Total CPU (ms)], qs.execution_count AS [Executions], CAST(qs.total_worker_time/qs.execution_count/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Avg CPU (ms)], CAST(qs.total_logical_reads/qs.execution_count AS BIGINT) AS [Avg Reads], qs.last_execution_time AS [Last Exec], LEFT(REPLACE(REPLACE(st.text,CHAR(13),' '),CHAR(10),' '),150) AS [SQL Text] 
,qp.query_plan [Plan]
FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(qs.sql_handle) st 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_query_plan(qs.plan_handle) AS qp
ORDER BY qs.total_worker_time DESC

panels.title: Top I/O Queries
panels.description: Top 20 queries by total logical reads since last restart.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT TOP 20 CAST(qs.total_logical_reads/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Total Reads (K)], qs.execution_count AS [Executions], CAST(qs.total_logical_reads/qs.execution_count AS BIGINT) AS [Avg Reads], CAST(qs.total_worker_time/qs.execution_count/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Avg CPU (ms)], qs.last_execution_time AS [Last Exec], LEFT(REPLACE(REPLACE(st.text,CHAR(13),' '),CHAR(10),' '),150) AS [SQL Text] 
,qp.query_plan [Plan]
FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(qs.sql_handle) st 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_query_plan(qs.plan_handle) AS qp
ORDER BY qs.total_logical_reads DESC

panels.title: Top Duration Queries
panels.description: Top 20 queries by total elapsed time since last restart.

query.sqlServer:
SELECT TOP 20 CAST(qs.total_elapsed_time/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Total Duration (ms)], qs.execution_count AS [Executions], CAST(qs.total_elapsed_time/qs.execution_count/1000.0 AS DECIMAL(18,1)) AS [Avg Duration (ms)], CAST(qs.total_logical_reads/qs.execution_count AS BIGINT) AS [Avg Reads], qs.last_execution_time AS [Last Exec], LEFT(REPLACE(REPLACE(st.text,CHAR(13),' '),CHAR(10),' '),150) AS [SQL Text] 
,qp.query_plan [Plan]
FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(qs.sql_handle) st 
OUTER APPLY sys.dm_exec_query_plan(qs.plan_handle) AS qp
ORDER BY qs.total_elapsed_time DESC

panels.title: TempDB Used (MB)
panels.description: Total TempDB space used by user objects, internal objects and version store.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(SUM(user_object_reserved_page_count+internal_object_reserved_page_count+version_store_reserved_page_count)*8/1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_db_file_space_usage

panels.title: Version Store (MB)
panels.description: TempDB space consumed by the version store. High values may indicate long-running transactions with RCSI/SI.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(SUM(version_store_reserved_page_count)*8/1024.0 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_db_file_space_usage

panels.title: Long Transactions (>5 min)
panels.description: Transactions open for more than 5 minutes. These can block version store cleanup.

query.sqlServer: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS FLOAT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_tran_active_transactions t JOIN sys.dm_tran_session_transactions st ON t.transaction_id = st.transaction_id WHERE DATEDIFF(MINUTE,t.transaction_begin_time,GETDATE()) > 5

panels.title: TempDB File Usage
panels.description: Per-file allocation, used and free space in TempDB.

query.sqlServer: SELECT f.name AS [File], f.physical_name AS [Path], CAST(f.size*8.0/1024 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Size MB], CAST((fs.user_object_reserved_page_count+fs.internal_object_reserved_page_count+fs.version_store_reserved_page_count)*8.0/1024 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Used MB], CAST(fs.unallocated_extent_page_count*8.0/1024 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Free MB] FROM tempdb.sys.dm_db_file_space_usage fs CROSS JOIN tempdb.sys.database_files f WHERE f.file_id = fs.file_id

panels.title: Top TempDB Users
panels.description: Sessions currently consuming the most TempDB space via user objects and internal objects.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 20 s.session_id AS [Session], s.login_name AS [Login], s.host_name AS [Host], s.program_name AS [Program], CAST((tsu.user_objects_alloc_page_count+tsu.internal_objects_alloc_page_count)*8.0/1024 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Alloc MB], CAST((tsu.user_objects_dealloc_page_count+tsu.internal_objects_dealloc_page_count)*8.0/1024 AS DECIMAL(10,1)) AS [Freed MB] FROM sys.dm_db_task_space_usage tsu JOIN sys.dm_exec_sessions s ON tsu.session_id = s.session_id WHERE tsu.user_objects_alloc_page_count+tsu.internal_objects_alloc_page_count > 0 ORDER BY tsu.user_objects_alloc_page_count+tsu.internal_objects_alloc_page_count DESC

panels.title: Long-Running Transactions
panels.description: Active transactions running for more than 5 minutes with session and login details.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 20 st.session_id AS [Session], t.transaction_begin_time AS [Started], DATEDIFF(MINUTE,t.transaction_begin_time,GETDATE()) AS [Duration (min)], t.transaction_type AS [Type], s.login_name AS [Login], s.host_name AS [Host], s.program_name AS [Program] FROM sys.dm_tran_active_transactions t JOIN sys.dm_tran_session_transactions st ON t.transaction_id = st.transaction_id JOIN sys.dm_exec_sessions s ON st.session_id = s.session_id WHERE DATEDIFF(MINUTE,t.transaction_begin_time,GETDATE()) > 5 ORDER BY t.transaction_begin_time ASC

SELECT CAST([value] AS INT) AS [Value]
FROM sys.configurations WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE name = 'max degree of parallelism'

SELECT SUM(work_queue_count) AS [Value]
FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE parent_node_id < 64 AND scheduler_id < 255

SELECT CASE WHEN sql_memory_model = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS [Value]
FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info WITH (NOLOCK)

SELECT COUNT(*) AS [Value]
FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE event_type IN (1, 2, 3)

SELECT COUNT(*) AS [Value]
FROM sys.sql_logins WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE is_policy_checked = 0 OR is_expiration_checked = 0

panels.title: Max Server Memory vs System RAM

query.sqlServer:
SELECT
    sc.[value]                                      AS [configured_max_mem_MB],
    dosi.physical_memory_kb / 1024                  AS [total_system_mem_MB],
    CAST(sc.[value] * 100.0
         / NULLIF(dosi.physical_memory_kb / 1024, 0)
         AS DECIMAL(5,2))                           AS [pct_of_system_mem],
    CASE
        WHEN sc.[value] = 2147483647
            THEN '[WARNING] MaxServerMemory is default — revise before memory pressure occurs'
        WHEN sc.[value] > dosi.physical_memory_kb / 1024
            THEN '[WARNING] MaxServerMemory exceeds total physical RAM'
        WHEN sc.[value] * 100.0 / NULLIF(dosi.physical_memory_kb / 1024, 0) > 90
            THEN '[CAUTION] MaxServerMemory leaves <10 % for OS — consider reducing'
        ELSE '[OK]'
    END                                             AS [recommendation]
FROM sys.configurations sc WITH (NOLOCK)
CROSS JOIN sys.dm_os_sys_info dosi WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE sc.name = 'max server memory (MB)'

SELECT
    DB_NAME(f.database_id)                                          AS [database_name],
    f.name                                                          AS [logical_file],
    f.type_desc                                                     AS [file_type],
    fs.num_of_reads                                                 AS [read_count],
    fs.num_of_writes                                                AS [write_count],
    CAST(fs.io_stall_read_ms  / (1.0 + fs.num_of_reads)  AS NUMERIC(10,2)) AS [avg_read_latency_ms],
    CAST(fs.io_stall_write_ms / (1.0 + fs.num_of_writes) AS NUMERIC(10,2)) AS [avg_write_latency_ms],
    fs.io_stall                                                     AS [total_stall_ms],
    CASE
        WHEN fs.io_stall_read_ms  / (1.0 + fs.num_of_reads)  >= 50
          OR fs.io_stall_write_ms / (1.0 + fs.num_of_writes) >= 50
            THEN '[WARNING] High latency — review storage configuration'
        WHEN fs.io_stall_read_ms  / (1.0 + fs.num_of_reads)  >= 20
          OR fs.io_stall_write_ms / (1.0 + fs.num_of_writes) >= 20
            THEN '[CAUTION] Elevated latency'
        ELSE '[OK]'
    END                                                             AS [status]
FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL) AS fs
INNER JOIN sys.master_files AS f WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON fs.database_id = f.database_id AND fs.file_id = f.file_id
ORDER BY fs.io_stall DESC

panels.title: Top Wait Stats by Category (cumulative since restart)

query.sqlServer:
;WITH Waits AS (
    SELECT
        wait_type,
        wait_time_ms / 1000.0                                                           AS wait_time_s,
        signal_wait_time_ms / 1000.0                                                    AS signal_wait_time_s,
        (wait_time_ms - signal_wait_time_ms) / 1000.0                                  AS resource_wait_time_s,
        100.0 * wait_time_ms / NULLIF(SUM(wait_time_ms) OVER (), 0)                   AS pct,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY wait_time_ms DESC)                                  AS rn
    FROM sys.dm_os_wait_stats WITH (NOLOCK)
    WHERE wait_type NOT IN (
        'RESOURCE_QUEUE','SQLTRACE_INCREMENTAL_FLUSH_SLEEP','SP_SERVER_DIAGNOSTICS_SLEEP',
        'SOSHOST_SLEEP','QDS_PERSIST_TASK_MAIN_LOOP_SLEEP','QDS_CLEANUP_STALE_QUERIES_TASK_MAIN_LOOP_SLEEP',
        'LOGMGR_QUEUE','CHECKPOINT_QUEUE','REQUEST_FOR_DEADLOCK_SEARCH','XE_TIMER_EVENT',
        'BROKER_TASK_STOP','CLR_MANUAL_EVENT','CLR_AUTO_EVENT','DISPATCHER_QUEUE_SEMAPHORE',
        'FT_IFTS_SCHEDULER_IDLE_WAIT','BROKER_TO_FLUSH','XE_DISPATCHER_WAIT','XE_DISPATCHER_JOIN',
        'MSQL_XP','WAIT_FOR_RESULTS','CLR_SEMAPHORE','LAZYWRITER_SLEEP','SLEEP_TASK',
        'SLEEP_SYSTEMTASK','SQLTRACE_BUFFER_FLUSH','WAITFOR','BROKER_EVENTHANDLER','TRACEWRITE',
        'FT_IFTSHC_MUTEX','BROKER_RECEIVE_WAITFOR','ONDEMAND_TASK_QUEUE','DBMIRROR_EVENTS_QUEUE',
        'DBMIRRORING_CMD','BROKER_TRANSMITTER','SQLTRACE_WAIT_ENTRIES','SLEEP_BPOOL_FLUSH',
        'SQLTRACE_LOCK','DIRTY_PAGE_POLL','HADR_FILESTREAM_IOMGR_IOCOMPLETION',
        'WAIT_XTP_OFFLINE_CKPT_NEW_LOG'
    )
    AND wait_type NOT LIKE 'SLEEP_%'
)
SELECT
    W1.wait_type,
    CAST(W1.wait_time_s        AS DECIMAL(14,2)) AS wait_time_s,
    CAST(W1.signal_wait_time_s AS DECIMAL(14,2)) AS signal_wait_time_s,
    CAST(W1.pct                AS DECIMAL(14,2)) AS pct,
    CASE
        WHEN W1.wait_type = 'SOS_SCHEDULER_YIELD'                              THEN 'CPU'
        WHEN W1.wait_type = 'THREADPOOL'                                       THEN 'CPU - Worker Threads'
        WHEN W1.wait_type IN ('CXPACKET','EXCHANGE','CXCONSUMER',
             'HTBUILD','HTDELETE','HTMEMO','HTREINIT','HTREPARTITION')         THEN 'CPU - Parallelism'
        WHEN W1.wait_type LIKE 'LCK_%' OR W1.wait_type = 'LOCK'               THEN 'Lock'
        WHEN W1.wait_type LIKE 'LATCH_%'                                       THEN 'Latch'
        WHEN W1.wait_type LIKE 'PAGELATCH_%'                                   THEN 'Buffer Latch'
        WHEN W1.wait_type LIKE 'PAGEIOLATCH_%'                                 THEN 'Buffer IO'
        WHEN W1.wait_type IN ('LOGMGR','LOGBUFFER','LOGMGR_FLUSH','WRITELOG') THEN 'Tran Log IO'
        WHEN W1.wait_type IN ('ASYNC_NETWORK_IO','NET_WAITFOR_PACKET')         THEN 'Network IO'
        WHEN W1.wait_type LIKE 'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE_%'                          THEN 'Memory - Compilation'
        WHEN W1.wait_type IN ('UTIL_PAGE_ALLOC','SOS_VIRTUALMEMORY_LOW',
             'SOS_RESERVEDMEMBLOCKLIST','RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE','CMEMTHREAD')     THEN 'Memory'
        ELSE 'Other'
    END AS wait_category
FROM Waits W1
WHERE W1.wait_time_s >= 0.01
ORDER BY W1.rn

SELECT
    SUM(current_tasks_count)   AS [current_tasks],
    SUM(runnable_tasks_count)  AS [runnable_tasks],
    SUM(work_queue_count)      AS [work_queue_count],
    SUM(pending_disk_io_count) AS [pending_disk_io],
    CASE
        WHEN SUM(work_queue_count) > 1
            THEN '[WARNING] Work queue > 1 — possible worker thread exhaustion'
        WHEN AVG(runnable_tasks_count) > 2
            THEN '[CAUTION] High runnable task count — CPU pressure'
        ELSE '[OK]'
    END AS [status]
FROM sys.dm_os_schedulers WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE parent_node_id < 64 AND scheduler_id < 255

SELECT
    DB_NAME(i.database_id)                                                          AS [database_name],
    OBJECT_NAME(i.object_id, i.database_id)                                         AS [table_name],
    i.equality_columns                                                              AS [equality_key_cols],
    i.inequality_columns                                                            AS [inequality_key_cols],
    i.included_columns                                                              AS [included_cols],
    s.user_seeks + s.user_scans                                                     AS [user_hits],
    CAST(s.avg_user_impact       AS DECIMAL(6,2))                                   AS [avg_impact_pct],
    CAST((s.user_seeks + s.user_scans)
         * s.avg_total_user_cost * s.avg_user_impact AS DECIMAL(18,0))              AS [score]
FROM sys.dm_db_missing_index_details i WITH (NOLOCK)
INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_groups g WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON i.index_handle = g.index_handle
INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_group_stats s WITH (NOLOCK)
    ON s.group_handle = g.index_group_handle
WHERE i.database_id > 4
  AND (s.user_seeks + s.user_scans) * s.avg_total_user_cost * s.avg_user_impact >= 100000
ORDER BY score DESC

SELECT
    name                    AS [login_name],
    is_policy_checked       AS [policy_checked],
    is_expiration_checked   AS [expiration_checked],
    LOGINPROPERTY(name, 'IsLocked')   AS [is_locked],
    LOGINPROPERTY(name, 'IsExpired')  AS [is_expired]
FROM sys.sql_logins WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE is_policy_checked = 0 OR is_expiration_checked = 0
ORDER BY name

SELECT
    DB_NAME(database_id) AS [database_name],
    file_id              AS [file_id],
    page_id              AS [page_id],
    CASE event_type
        WHEN 1 THEN 'Error 823 / unspecified 824'
        WHEN 2 THEN 'Bad Checksum'
        WHEN 3 THEN 'Torn Page'
        ELSE CAST(event_type AS VARCHAR(10))
    END                  AS [event_type],
    error_count          AS [error_count],
    last_update_date     AS [last_update_date]
FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WITH (NOLOCK)
WHERE event_type IN (1, 2, 3)
ORDER BY DB_NAME(database_id), last_update_date DESC, file_id, page_id

panels.title: Publications
panels.description: All publications configured on this server.

query.sqlServer: SELECT p.name AS [Publication], DB_NAME(p.database_id) AS [Database], CASE p.publication_type WHEN 0 THEN 'Transactional' WHEN 1 THEN 'Snapshot' WHEN 2 THEN 'Merge' ELSE CAST(p.publication_type AS VARCHAR(5)) END AS [Type], CASE p.status WHEN 0 THEN 'Inactive' WHEN 1 THEN 'Active' ELSE CAST(p.status AS VARCHAR(5)) END AS [Status], p.immediate_sync AS [ImmediateSync], p.allow_push AS [AllowPush], p.allow_pull AS [AllowPull] FROM distribution.dbo.MSpublications p WITH (NOLOCK) ORDER BY p.name

panels.title: Subscriptions
panels.description: Subscription status across all publications.

query.sqlServer: SELECT p.name AS [Publication], s.subscriber_db AS [SubscriberDB], ss.name AS [Subscriber], CASE s.status WHEN 0 THEN 'Inactive' WHEN 1 THEN 'Subscribed' WHEN 2 THEN 'Active' ELSE CAST(s.status AS VARCHAR(5)) END AS [Status], CASE s.subscription_type WHEN 0 THEN 'Push' WHEN 1 THEN 'Pull' ELSE '?' END AS [Type], s.sync_type AS [SyncType] FROM distribution.dbo.MSsubscriptions s WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN distribution.dbo.MSpublications p WITH (NOLOCK) ON s.publication_id = p.publication_id INNER JOIN sys.servers ss WITH (NOLOCK) ON s.subscriber_id = ss.server_id ORDER BY p.name, ss.name

panels.title: Undistributed Commands
panels.description: Commands waiting in the distribution database per subscription. High values indicate latency.

query.sqlServer: SELECT p.name AS [Publication], a.publisher_db AS [PublisherDB], ss.name AS [Subscriber], a.subscriber_db AS [SubscriberDB], SUM(CASE WHEN xact_seqno > delivered_seqno THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS [Undistributed], MAX(a.entry_time) AS [OldestEntry] FROM distribution.dbo.MSdistribution_status a WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN distribution.dbo.MSsubscriptions s WITH (NOLOCK) ON a.agent_id = s.agent_id INNER JOIN distribution.dbo.MSpublications p WITH (NOLOCK) ON s.publication_id = p.publication_id INNER JOIN sys.servers ss WITH (NOLOCK) ON s.subscriber_id = ss.server_id GROUP BY p.name, a.publisher_db, ss.name, a.subscriber_db ORDER BY [Undistributed] DESC

panels.title: Replication Agent History
panels.description: Recent log reader and distribution agent activity.

query.sqlServer: SELECT TOP 50 a.name AS [Agent], CASE a.agent_type WHEN 1 THEN 'Snapshot' WHEN 2 THEN 'Log Reader' WHEN 3 THEN 'Distribution' WHEN 4 THEN 'Merge' ELSE CAST(a.agent_type AS VARCHAR(5)) END AS [Type], h.time AS [Time], h.comments AS [Message], h.duration AS [Duration(s)], h.delivered_commands AS [Commands], h.delivered_transactions AS [Transactions], CASE h.runstatus WHEN 1 THEN 'Start' WHEN 2 THEN 'Succeed' WHEN 3 THEN 'In Progress' WHEN 4 THEN 'Idle' WHEN 5 THEN 'Retry' WHEN 6 THEN 'Fail' ELSE CAST(h.runstatus AS VARCHAR(5)) END AS [Status] FROM distribution.dbo.MSdistribution_history h WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN distribution.dbo.MSdistribution_agents a WITH (NOLOCK) ON h.agent_id = a.id ORDER BY h.time DESC

panels.title: AG Health Summary
panels.description: Overall health state of each availability group.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ag.name AS [AG Name], ags.primary_replica AS [Primary], CASE ags.primary_recovery_health_desc WHEN 'ONLINE' THEN 'Healthy' ELSE ISNULL(ags.primary_recovery_health_desc, 'N/A') END AS [PrimaryHealth], ags.synchronization_health_desc AS [SyncHealth], CASE ags.synchronization_health WHEN 0 THEN 'Not Healthy' WHEN 1 THEN 'Partially Healthy' WHEN 2 THEN 'Healthy' END AS [OverallHealth] FROM sys.availability_groups ag WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.dm_hadr_availability_group_states ags WITH (NOLOCK) ON ag.group_id = ags.group_id ORDER BY ag.name

panels.title: Replica Status
panels.description: Per-replica availability mode, failover mode, connection state, and role.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ag.name AS [AG Name], ar.replica_server_name AS [Replica], ars.role_desc AS [Role], ar.availability_mode_desc AS [AvailabilityMode], ar.failover_mode_desc AS [FailoverMode], ars.connected_state_desc AS [ConnectionState], ars.synchronization_health_desc AS [SyncHealth], ars.operational_state_desc AS [OperationalState], ars.last_connect_error_number AS [LastErrorNo], ars.last_connect_error_description AS [LastError] FROM sys.availability_replicas ar WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.availability_groups ag WITH (NOLOCK) ON ar.group_id = ag.group_id INNER JOIN sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states ars WITH (NOLOCK) ON ar.replica_id = ars.replica_id ORDER BY ag.name, ars.role_desc DESC, ar.replica_server_name

panels.title: Database Sync Status
panels.description: Per-database synchronization state including redo queue, send queue, and log rates.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ag.name AS [AG Name], ar.replica_server_name AS [Replica], DB_NAME(drs.database_id) AS [Database], drs.synchronization_state_desc AS [SyncState], drs.synchronization_health_desc AS [SyncHealth], drs.is_suspended AS [Suspended], CAST(drs.log_send_queue_size AS BIGINT) AS [SendQueue(KB)], CAST(drs.redo_queue_size AS BIGINT) AS [RedoQueue(KB)], CAST(drs.log_send_rate AS BIGINT) AS [SendRate(KB/s)], CAST(drs.redo_rate AS BIGINT) AS [RedoRate(KB/s)], drs.last_sent_time AS [LastSent], drs.last_received_time AS [LastReceived], drs.last_hardened_time AS [LastHardened], drs.last_redone_time AS [LastRedone] FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states drs WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.availability_replicas ar WITH (NOLOCK) ON drs.replica_id = ar.replica_id INNER JOIN sys.availability_groups ag WITH (NOLOCK) ON ar.group_id = ag.group_id ORDER BY ag.name, ar.replica_server_name, DB_NAME(drs.database_id)

panels.title: Send Queue Trend
panels.description: Log send queue size over time across all AG databases. Rising trends indicate network or throughput issues.

query.sqlServer: SELECT GETDATE() AS [Time], ar.replica_server_name + ' / ' + DB_NAME(drs.database_id) AS [Series], CAST(drs.log_send_queue_size AS BIGINT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states drs WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.availability_replicas ar WITH (NOLOCK) ON drs.replica_id = ar.replica_id WHERE drs.is_local = 0 ORDER BY [Value] DESC

panels.title: Redo Queue Trend
panels.description: Redo queue size over time. High values mean the secondary is falling behind.

query.sqlServer: SELECT GETDATE() AS [Time], ar.replica_server_name + ' / ' + DB_NAME(drs.database_id) AS [Series], CAST(drs.redo_queue_size AS BIGINT) AS [Value] FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states drs WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.availability_replicas ar WITH (NOLOCK) ON drs.replica_id = ar.replica_id WHERE drs.is_local = 0 ORDER BY [Value] DESC

panels.title: AG Listeners
panels.description: Availability group listener endpoints and IP configuration.

query.sqlServer: SELECT ag.name AS [AG Name], l.dns_name AS [Listener], l.port AS [Port], lip.ip_address AS [IP Address], lip.ip_subnet_mask AS [Subnet], lip.state_desc AS [State] FROM sys.availability_group_listeners l WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN sys.availability_groups ag WITH (NOLOCK) ON l.group_id = ag.group_id INNER JOIN sys.availability_group_listener_ip_addresses lip WITH (NOLOCK) ON l.listener_id = lip.listener_id ORDER BY ag.name, l.dns_name

panels.title: Running Jobs
panels.description: Currently executing SQL Agent jobs.

query.sqlServer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS [Value] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity ja WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) ON ja.job_id = j.job_id WHERE ja.session_id = (SELECT MAX(session_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity WITH (NOLOCK)) AND ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL

panels.title: Failed (24h)
panels.description: Jobs that failed in the last 24 hours.

query.sqlServer: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT j.name) AS [Value] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) ON h.job_id = j.job_id WHERE h.step_id = 0 AND h.run_status = 0 AND msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time) >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE())

panels.title: Succeeded (24h)
panels.description: Jobs that completed successfully in the last 24 hours.

query.sqlServer: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT j.name) AS [Value] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) ON h.job_id = j.job_id WHERE h.step_id = 0 AND h.run_status = 1 AND msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time) >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE())

panels.title: Disabled Jobs
panels.description: Total number of disabled agent jobs.

query.sqlServer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS [Value] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs WITH (NOLOCK) WHERE enabled = 0

panels.title: Running Jobs
panels.description: Currently executing SQL Agent jobs with start time and duration.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job], ja.start_execution_date AS [Started], DATEDIFF(MINUTE,ja.start_execution_date,GETDATE()) AS [Running (min)], js.step_name AS [Current Step] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity ja JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j ON ja.job_id = j.job_id LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobsteps js ON ja.job_id = js.job_id AND ja.last_executed_step_id = js.step_id WHERE ja.session_id = (SELECT MAX(session_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity) AND ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL ORDER BY ja.start_execution_date

panels.title: All Jobs — Last Outcome
panels.description: Every SQL Agent job with its last run status, duration, and next scheduled run.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job Name], CASE j.enabled WHEN 1 THEN 'Enabled' ELSE 'Disabled' END AS [Status], CASE WHEN ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL THEN 'Running' WHEN h.run_status = 0 THEN 'Failed' WHEN h.run_status = 1 THEN 'Succeeded' WHEN h.run_status = 2 THEN 'Retry' WHEN h.run_status = 3 THEN 'Cancelled' ELSE 'Unknown' END AS [LastOutcome], CASE WHEN ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL THEN CONVERT(VARCHAR, ja.start_execution_date, 120) WHEN h.run_date IS NOT NULL THEN CONVERT(VARCHAR, msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time), 120) ELSE '' END AS [LastRun], CASE WHEN h.run_duration IS NOT NULL THEN RIGHT('0' + CAST(h.run_duration / 10000 AS VARCHAR), 2) + ':' + RIGHT('0' + CAST((h.run_duration / 100) % 100 AS VARCHAR), 2) + ':' + RIGHT('0' + CAST(h.run_duration % 100 AS VARCHAR), 2) ELSE '' END AS [Duration], CASE WHEN ja.next_scheduled_run_date IS NOT NULL THEN CONVERT(VARCHAR, ja.next_scheduled_run_date, 120) ELSE '' END AS [NextRun], c.name AS [Category] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.syscategories c WITH (NOLOCK) ON j.category_id = c.category_id LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity ja WITH (NOLOCK) ON j.job_id = ja.job_id AND ja.session_id = (SELECT MAX(session_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity WITH (NOLOCK)) OUTER APPLY (SELECT TOP 1 run_status, run_date, run_time, run_duration FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory WITH (NOLOCK) WHERE job_id = j.job_id AND step_id = 0 ORDER BY run_date DESC, run_time DESC) h ORDER BY j.name

panels.title: Recent Failures — Detail
panels.description: Step-level detail for failed job executions in the last 24 hours.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job Name], h.step_id AS [Step], h.step_name AS [StepName], CONVERT(VARCHAR, msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time), 120) AS [RunTime], RIGHT('0' + CAST(h.run_duration / 10000 AS VARCHAR), 2) + ':' + RIGHT('0' + CAST((h.run_duration / 100) % 100 AS VARCHAR), 2) + ':' + RIGHT('0' + CAST(h.run_duration % 100 AS VARCHAR), 2) AS [Duration], h.sql_severity AS [Severity], REPLACE(REPLACE(h.message, CHAR(13), ' '), CHAR(10), ' ') AS [Message] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) ON h.job_id = j.job_id WHERE h.run_status = 0 AND msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time) >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE()) ORDER BY msdb.dbo.agent_datetime(h.run_date, h.run_time) DESC

panels.title: Long Running Jobs (Now)
panels.description: Currently running jobs with their elapsed time.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job Name], ja.start_execution_date AS [Started], DATEDIFF(MINUTE, ja.start_execution_date, GETDATE()) AS [ElapsedMin], ISNULL(ja.last_executed_step_id, 0) AS [CurrentStep] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity ja WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) ON ja.job_id = j.job_id WHERE ja.session_id = (SELECT MAX(session_id) FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobactivity WITH (NOLOCK)) AND ja.start_execution_date IS NOT NULL AND ja.stop_execution_date IS NULL ORDER BY ja.start_execution_date

panels.title: Job Schedules
panels.description: Active schedules for all enabled jobs.

query.sqlServer: SELECT j.name AS [Job Name], s.name AS [Schedule], CASE s.freq_type WHEN 1 THEN 'Once' WHEN 4 THEN 'Daily' WHEN 8 THEN 'Weekly' WHEN 16 THEN 'Monthly' WHEN 32 THEN 'Monthly Relative' WHEN 64 THEN 'Agent Start' WHEN 128 THEN 'Idle' ELSE CAST(s.freq_type AS VARCHAR(10)) END AS [Frequency], CASE WHEN s.freq_type = 4 THEN 'Every ' + CAST(s.freq_interval AS VARCHAR) + ' day(s)' WHEN s.freq_type = 8 THEN 'Every ' + CAST(s.freq_recurrence_factor AS VARCHAR) + ' week(s)' WHEN s.freq_type = 16 THEN 'Day ' + CAST(s.freq_interval AS VARCHAR) + ' every ' + CAST(s.freq_recurrence_factor AS VARCHAR) + ' month(s)' ELSE '' END AS [Detail], CASE WHEN s.active_start_date > 0 THEN CONVERT(VARCHAR, CONVERT(DATETIME, CAST(s.active_start_date AS VARCHAR(8)), 112), 120) ELSE '' END AS [StartDate], CASE s.enabled WHEN 1 THEN 'Enabled' ELSE 'Disabled' END AS [ScheduleStatus] FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j WITH (NOLOCK) INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobschedules js WITH (NOLOCK) ON j.job_id = js.job_id INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysschedules s WITH (NOLOCK) ON js.schedule_id = s.schedule_id WHERE j.enabled = 1 ORDER BY j.name, s.name
